   195→  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
   196→  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
   197→  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
   198→  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
   199→  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
   200→  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
   201→  - ✅ Responsive scrolling and empty state handling
   202→  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (Board/List/Timeline toggle)
   203→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   204→  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, children don't auto-adjust
   205→     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
   206→     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" - blocked tasks automatically adjust dates
   207→     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior, cascading date updates
   208→     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling required defeats purpose of dependencies - baseline in ALL competitors
   209→  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
   210→     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
   211→     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature, shows longest sequence
   212→     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature, red highlighting
   213→     - **Impact:** Can't optimize project timeline or identify delays - professional PM baseline
   214→  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
   215→     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot, green/red variance indicators
   216→     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with variance tracking
   217→     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or slippage - project management necessity
   218→  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
   219→     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress percentage within bars
   220→     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion display
   221→     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (filled portion)
   222→     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance - baseline feature
   223→  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines on timeline
   224→     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with diamond markers
   225→     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as specialized tasks
   226→     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers on timeline
   227→     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates/deliverables - essential for project planning
   228→  6. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only simple blocks/blocked-by
   229→     - MS Project: ✅ 4 dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag time
   230→     - **Note:** Type definitions EXIST in `src/types/index.ts` (TaskDependency interface with DependencyType enum) but only SimpleTaskDependency is implemented
   231→     - **Impact:** Can't model complex project relationships (parallel tasks, delayed starts)
   232→  7. ❌ **Subitem Support on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on Gantt
   233→     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems visible on timeline
   234→     - MS Project: ✅ Summary tasks with subtask rollup
   235→     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown hierarchy visually
   236→  8. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export Gantt to PDF/Excel/image
   237→     - Monday.com: ✅ Export to Excel
   238→     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF, Excel, images
   239→     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
   240→- **Our Unique Advantages:**
   241→  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
   242→  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
   243→  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
   244→  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
   245→  - ✅ Lightweight (no heavy dependencies)
   246→- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
   247→- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift, Critical Path, Baseline), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones, Professional Dependencies), **P2** (Export, Subitem support)
   248→- **Verdict:** MVP-level Gantt with unique swimlane visualization, but missing professional project management capabilities
   249→- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt Chart](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)
   250→
   251→🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   252→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   253→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, Asana, ClickUp
   254→- **Current Features:**
   255→  - ✅ Frequency patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) with intervals
   256→  - ✅ Weekly: Days of week selection (M/T/W/Th/F/Sa/Su checkboxes)
   257→  - ✅ Monthly: Day of month specification
   258→  - ✅ End conditions: Never / After N occurrences / Until specific date
   259→  - ✅ Auto-generation of next instance when current is completed
   260→  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation and display
   261→  - ✅ Parent-instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
   262→  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component with all options
   263→  - ✅ Integration with Kanban CardDetailPanel
   264→  - ✅ Instance management (view all instances of recurring task)
   265→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   266→  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Only recurs from original due date
   267→     - Todoist: ✅ "every day" (from due) vs "every! day" (from completion)
   268→     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur from due date" vs "Recur from completion date" toggle
   269→     - Asana: ✅ Same behavior with toggle
   270→     - **Impact:** Flexible tasks (e.g., "call client every 5 days") can't be modeled - major use case gap
   271→  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every 2 weeks on Monday" parser
   272→     - Todoist: ✅ Smart parser ("every weekday", "every 2 weeks", "every month on the 15th")
   273→     - ClickUp: ✅ Natural language + visual picker hybrid
   274→     - **Impact:** Slower to set up, requires clicking through UI - UX friction
   275→  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't pre-fill checklists/description
   276→     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use templates (checklists, custom fields, etc.)
   277→     - Asana: ✅ Task templates can be recurring
   278→     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup work for complex recurring tasks (e.g., weekly report with same checklist)
   279→  4. ⚠️ **Advanced Patterns** - No "last Friday of month" or "2nd Tuesday"
   280→     - Todoist: ✅ "every last friday" pattern
   281→     - **Impact:** Some business patterns can't be expressed (e.g., monthly all-hands meeting on first Monday)
   282→- **Our Advantages:**
   283→  - ✅ Full UI integration (not hidden in advanced settings)
   284→  - ✅ Instance relationship tracking (can see all occurrences)
   285→  - ✅ Free (Todoist advanced recurrence requires Premium)
   286→  - ✅ Offline-first
   287→- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 362-424)
   288→- **Priority:** **P1** (Recur from completion - high user value), **P2** (Natural language, Template integration), **P3** (Advanced patterns)
   289→- **Verdict:** Solid recurrence engine with all core patterns, but missing flexibility for real-world use cases
   290→- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://todoist.com/help/articles/set-a-recurring-due-date-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6310449699351-Recurring-Tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://help.asana.com/s/article/recurring-tasks?language=en_US)
   291→
   292→🔶 **Task Dependencies** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   293→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   294→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Asana, ClickUp, Monday.com
   295→- **Current Implementation:**
   296→  - ✅ SimpleTaskDependency: Basic blocks/blocked-by relationships
   297→  - ✅ UI in CardDetailPanel (lines 615-750): Add blocker/blocked tasks with dropdowns
   298→  - ✅ Visual indicators: ⚠️ Blocked By (yellow), 🔒 Blocks (blue accent)
   299→  - ✅ Click dependency to navigate to related task
   300→  - ✅ Remove dependencies with × button on hover
   301→  - ✅ Circular dependency prevention in addDependency()
   302→  - ✅ Self-dependency prevention
   303→  - ✅ Validation: Can't complete task with incomplete blockers (useKanbanStore.ts:274-287)
   304→  - ✅ Activity log tracking for dependency changes
   305→  - ✅ Gantt timeline visualization (DependencyLine.tsx with SVG arrows)
   306→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   307→  1. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only Finish-to-Start implemented
   308→     - **IMPORTANT:** Full TaskDependency interface DEFINED in `src/types/index.ts` but NOT USED:
   309→       ```typescript
   310→       export type DependencyType = 'finish-to-start' | 'start-to-start' | 'finish-to-finish' | 'start-to-finish';
   311→       export interface TaskDependency {
   312→         taskId: string;
   313→         type: DependencyType;
   314→         lag: number; // Lag time in days (can be negative for lead)
   315→       }
   316→       ```
   317→     - Asana: ✅ Basic dependencies only (similar to our current)
   318→     - ClickUp: ✅ 4 types + waiting on/blocking
   319→     - MS Project: ✅ All 4 types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag/lead time
   320→     - **Impact:** Can't model parallel work (SS), coordinated finishes (FF), or complex schedules - professional PM baseline
   321→  2. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - Date changes don't cascade (already noted in Gantt audit)
   322→     - ClickUp: ✅ "Rescheduling dependencies" option
   323→     - MS Project: ✅ Automatic cascading by default
   324→     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling burden, defeats dependency purpose
   325→  3. ❌ **Lag/Lead Time** - No "+3 days after" or "-2 days before"
   326→     - MS Project: ✅ Lag time in dependency definition
   327→     - **Impact:** Can't express "start QA 2 days before dev finishes" - real-world scheduling need
   328→  4. ❌ **Dependency Warnings** - No alerts when blocker is overdue
   329→     - ClickUp: ✅ Warning badges when blocked by overdue tasks
   330→     - Monday.com: ✅ Dependency status indicators
   331→     - **Impact:** Silent failures, tasks stuck without visibility
   332→- **Quick Win Opportunity:** TaskDependency types already defined, just need to:
   333→  1. Add type selector to CardDetailPanel dependency UI
   334→  2. Update addDependency() to accept type + lag parameters
   335→  3. Implement cascading date logic in GanttTimeline
   336→  4. Add lag time input to dependency creation
   337→- **Files:** `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 1019-1105), `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx` (lines 615-750), `src/types/index.ts` (lines 53-63, 98-103), `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
   338→- **Priority:** **P0** (Professional types + lag - types already defined!), **P1** (Auto-shift, Warnings)
   339→- **Verdict:** Basic dependency tracking works, but professional PM features are defined but unimplemented - low-hanging fruit
   340→- **Sources:** [Asana Dependencies](https://help.asana.com/s/article/dependencies?language=en_US), [ClickUp Task Dependencies](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305955166359-Task-Dependencies), [MS Project Dependencies](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/link-tasks-in-a-project-d7c17100-6dc7-49b6-b5f1-30ed86dbd079)
   341→
   342→🔶 **Custom Fields** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   343→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   344→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com, ClickUp, Airtable
   345→- **Current Implementation:**
   346→  - ✅ 5 field types: text, number, date, select, checkbox (src/types/customFields.ts)
   347→  - ✅ FieldDefinition schema: id, name, type, defaultValue, options, required, description, timestamps
   348→  - ✅ Field validation with validateFieldValue() function
   349→  - ✅ CustomFieldEditor component for rendering appropriate inputs (src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx)
   350→  - ✅ Accessibility: ARIA labels, required indicators, error messaging
   351→  - ✅ Semantic styling with Tailwind tokens
   352→  - ✅ Integration with useSettingsStore for field definitions
   353→  - ✅ CustomFieldsMap type for storing values on tasks/notes
   354→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   355→  1. ❌ **Field Management UI** - No interface to create/edit/delete field definitions
   356→     - Monday.com: ✅ Column center for adding/managing custom columns
   357→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom fields manager with templates
   358→     - Airtable: ✅ Field type selector with full customization
   359→     - **Impact:** Users can't actually USE the custom fields system - implementation is backend-only
   360→  2. ❌ **Additional Field Types** - Missing common types
   361→     - Monday.com: ✅ Also has: Person, Status, Timeline, Rating, File, Link, Formula
   362→     - ClickUp: ✅ Also has: Dropdown (multi-select), Progress, Labels, Relationships, Email, Phone, URL
   363→     - Airtable: ✅ Also has: Multi-select, Long text, Attachment, URL, Email, Phone, Rating, Barcode, Formula, Rollup, Lookup
   364→     - **Impact:** Can't model real-world data (file uploads, user assignment, calculations)
   365→  3. ❌ **Field Display in Lists** - Custom fields not shown in Kanban card list/board views
   366→     - Monday.com: ✅ Custom columns visible in board view
   367→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom fields show in list/board with toggles
   368→     - **Impact:** Fields are hidden unless you open card detail - low visibility
   369→  4. ❌ **Field Templates** - No reusable field sets
   370→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom field templates (e.g., "Bug Report" field set)
   371→     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup for similar task types
   372→  5. ❌ **Formulas & Rollups** - No computed fields
   373→     - Airtable: ✅ Formula fields, rollup from linked records
   374→     - Monday.com: ✅ Formula columns
   375→     - **Impact:** Can't auto-calculate totals, averages, or derive values
   376→- **Our Foundation Strengths:**
   377→  - ✅ Type-safe field definitions with Zod validation
   378→  - ✅ Reusable CustomFieldEditor component
   379→  - ✅ Accessible UI patterns established
   380→  - ✅ Clean separation of concerns (types, validation, rendering)
   381→- **Files:** `src/types/customFields.ts`, `src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx`, `src/components/CustomFieldsSettings.tsx`, `src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts`
   382→- **Priority:** **P0** (Field Management UI - without it, feature is unusable), **P1** (Display in views, Additional types), **P2** (Templates, Formulas)
   383→- **Verdict:** Well-architected foundation with NO user-facing interface - feature is 90% invisible
   384→- **Sources:** [Monday.com Custom Columns](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005348365-Custom-Columns), [ClickUp Custom Fields](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305790296087-Custom-Fields), [Airtable Field Types](https://www.airtable.com/guides/start/field-types)
   385→
   386→### Notes & Knowledge Management
   387→🔶 **Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   388→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   389→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
   390→- **Current Implementation:**
   391→  - ✅ Wiki-style link syntax: `[[Note Title]]` (LINK_PATTERN regex in backlinks.ts)
   392→  - ✅ Link extraction from note content (`extractWikiLinks()`)
   393→  - ✅ Link resolution: Exact + partial title matching (`resolveLinksToIds()`)
   394→  - ✅ Backlinks panel component (BacklinksPanel.tsx) with count badge
   395→  - ✅ Click backlink to navigate to source note
   396→  - ✅ Empty state: "Use [[Note Title]] to create links" instruction
   397→  - ✅ Auto-update linked notes when content changes (`updateLinkedNotes()`)
   398→  - ✅ linkedNotes array stored on Note type
   399→  - ✅ Backlinks panel integrated in NotesEditor
   400→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   401→  1. ❌ **Unlinked Mentions** - No detection of note titles mentioned without brackets
   402→     - Obsidian: ✅ "Unlinked mentions" section showing implicit references
   403→     - Roam: ✅ Detects unlinked references automatically
   404→     - Logseq: ✅ Shows both linked and unlinked references
   405→     - **Impact:** Miss implicit connections, manual linking burden
   406→  2. ❌ **Link Autocomplete** - No `[[` trigger for note picker
   407→     - Obsidian: ✅ Type `[[` to open note search popup
   408→     - Roam: ✅ Instant search on `[[` with fuzzy matching
   409→     - Logseq: ✅ Page autocomplete on `[[`
   410→     - **Impact:** Must remember exact titles, typos create broken links
   411→  3. ❌ **Broken Link Detection** - No warnings for links to non-existent notes
   412→     - Obsidian: ✅ Purple color for broken links, "Create note" option
   413→     - Roam: ✅ Auto-creates page on click
   414→     - Logseq: ✅ Broken link styling + creation option
   415→     - **Impact:** Silent failures, unclear if link is valid
   416→  4. ❌ **Block-level Links** - Can't link to specific paragraphs/headings
   417→     - Obsidian: ✅ `[[Note#Heading]]` and `[[Note^block-id]]` syntax
   418→     - Roam: ✅ Block references with `((block-id))`
   419→     - Logseq: ✅ Block embedding and references
   420→     - **Impact:** Must link to entire note, not specific sections
   421→  5. ❌ **Link Preview on Hover** - No popover showing linked note content
   422→     - Obsidian: ✅ Hover preview with note excerpt
   423→     - Roam: ✅ Inline preview on hover
   424→     - **Impact:** Must click links to see content, slows research flow
   425→- **Our Advantages:**
   426→  - ✅ Partial matching (finds "JavaScript Basics" when linking "JavaScript")
   427→  - ✅ Clean UI with count badges
   428→  - ✅ Offline-first
   429→- **Files:** `src/utils/backlinks.ts`, `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (line 79), `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
   430→- **Priority:** **P0** (Link autocomplete - UX blocker), **P1** (Unlinked mentions, Broken link detection), **P2** (Block links, Hover preview)
   431→- **Verdict:** Basic bidirectional linking works, but missing PKM essentials for serious knowledge work
   432→- **Sources:** [Obsidian Linking](https://help.obsidian.md/Linking+notes+and+files/Internal+links), [Roam Research Bidirectional Links](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/Roam_Research_White_Paper), [Logseq Backlinks](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/contents)
   433→
   434→🔶 **Notes - Graph View** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   435→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   436→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
   437→- **Current Implementation:**
   438→  - ✅ D3 force-directed graph (GraphCanvas.tsx using d3.forceSimulation)
   439→  - ✅ Node types: Notes (circles) and Tags (differentiated)
   440→  - ✅ Edge types: Backlinks (cyan arrows), Tag relationships
   441→  - ✅ Zoom/pan with d3.zoom (scale 0.1-4x)
   442→  - ✅ Drag nodes to reposition (with physics simulation)
   443→  - ✅ Click node to navigate (onNodeClick callback)
   444→  - ✅ Physics forces: collision, charge (repulsion), centering, link distance
   445→  - ✅ Arrow markers for directed edges
   446→  - ✅ Color coding: backlinks (cyan), regular edges (border color)
   447→  - ✅ SVG-based rendering for performance
   448→  - ✅ Graph data processing utilities (graphDataProcessor.ts)
   449→  - ✅ Dedicated /graph-view page
   450→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   451→  1. ❌ **Local Graph** - No focused view for single note's connections
   452→     - Obsidian: ✅ Local graph showing 1-2 hop neighbors
   453→     - Roam: ✅ Page graph for current page
   454→     - Logseq: ✅ Local graph with depth slider
   455→     - **Impact:** Can't focus on specific note's neighborhood, overwhelming for large graphs
   456→  2. ❌ **Filters** - No ability to filter by tags, folders, date ranges
   457→     - Obsidian: ✅ Filter by tags, folders, paths, search terms
   458→     - Logseq: ✅ Filter by page properties, tags, namespaces
   459→     - **Impact:** Graph becomes unusable with 100+ notes, no way to focus
   460→  3. ❌ **Node Sizing** - All nodes same size, no indication of importance
   461→     - Obsidian: ✅ Node size = number of connections (degree centrality)
   462→     - Roam: ✅ Node size based on reference count
   463→     - **Impact:** Can't identify hub notes or important concepts visually
   464→  4. ❌ **Link Strength** - No indication of connection frequency
   465→     - Obsidian: ✅ Link thickness = number of references
   466→     - **Impact:** All connections appear equal weight
   467→  5. ❌ **Color Groups** - No automatic clustering or coloring by category
   468→     - Obsidian: ✅ Color groups by tags, folders, or custom groups
   469→     - Logseq: ✅ Namespace-based coloring
   470→     - **Impact:** Hard to see thematic clusters in knowledge base
   471→  6. ❌ **Orphan Detection** - No highlighting of isolated notes
   472→     - Obsidian: ✅ Shows orphan notes (no connections)
   473→     - **Impact:** Can't identify disconnected knowledge
   474→  7. ❌ **Search in Graph** - No search to highlight specific nodes
   475→     - Obsidian: ✅ Search bar highlights matching nodes
   476→     - **Impact:** Must visually scan to find notes
   477→- **Our Advantages:**
   478→  - ✅ Tag relationships visualized (not just note-to-note)
   479→  - ✅ Smooth physics simulation
   480→  - ✅ Free (comparable to Obsidian which is also free for local graphs)
   481→- **Files:** `src/components/Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx`, `src/utils/graphSimulation.ts`, `src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts`, `src/pages/GraphView.tsx`
   482→- **Priority:** **P0** (Local graph, Filters - unusable without these at scale), **P1** (Node sizing, Color groups), **P2** (Link strength, Search, Orphans)
   483→- **Verdict:** Beautiful graph visualization, but impractical for actual knowledge management at scale
   484→- **Sources:** [Obsidian Graph View](https://help.obsidian.md/Plugins/Graph+view), [Logseq Graph](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/graph%20view), [Roam Graph](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/graph-view)
   485→
   486→🔶 **Notes - Tags System** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   487→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   488→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Bear, Notion, Obsidian
   489→- **Current Implementation:**
   490→  - ✅ Array of string tags on Note type (`tags: string[]`)
   491→  - ✅ TagPicker component with autocomplete (TagPicker.tsx)
   492→  - ✅ Tag filtering in Notes page
   493→  - ✅ Tag display on note cards
   494→  - ✅ Free-text tag creation
   495→  - ✅ Tags integrated with Graph View (tag nodes + relationships)
   496→  - ✅ Daily notes auto-tags: `daily-note`, `{year}`, `month-{MM}`
   497→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   498→  1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
   499→     - Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator

# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)
2. [Completed Features (100% Parity)](#completed-features-100-parity)
3. [Partial Implementation (<100% Parity)](#partial-implementation-100-parity)
4. [Not Started](#not-started)
5. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
6. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)
7. [Competitor Feature Matrix](#competitor-feature-matrix)
8. [Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)](#provider-directory--ecosystem-map-2025)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Assignees/Members** - Cannot assign tasks to team members
     - Trello: ✅ Multiple assignees per card
     - Asana: ✅ Single assignee + followers
     - Monday.com: ✅ Multiple people per task
     - **Impact:** MAJOR blocker for team collaboration - baseline feature in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **File Attachments** - Cannot attach files to cards
     - Trello: ✅ Drag-drop images/docs, cloud storage integration
     - Asana: ✅ File attachments with preview
     - Monday.com: ✅ File columns with covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Limits use case to simple text-only tasks - baseline in ALL competitors
  3. ❌ **WIP Limits** - Cannot set column task limits
     - Monday.com: ✅ Task limits per column with warnings
     - Trello: Available via Power-Up
     - **Impact:** Missing key Kanban methodology feature
  4. ❌ **Card Covers/Images** - No visual covers on cards
     - Trello: ✅ Image covers from attachments
     - Monday.com: ✅ File column covers (crop/fit modes)
     - **Impact:** Less visual, harder to scan board at a glance
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes
  - ✅ Better keyboard shortcuts than competitors
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage (privacy advantage)
  - ✅ No paywalls (Timeline/Gantt included for free)
  - ✅ Faster performance (60KB vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Files:** `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanColumn.tsx`, `src/widgets/Kanban/ListView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Assignees, File Attachments), **P1** (WIP Limits, Card Covers)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Kanban with excellent offline/keyboard UX, but missing baseline team collaboration features
- **Sources:** [Trello Kanban Features](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/trello-kanban-board), [Asana Board View](https://help.asana.com/s/article/board-view?language=en_US), [Monday.com Kanban](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360000661379-The-Kanban-View)

🔶 **Timeline/Gantt View** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com Gantt Chart, Asana Gantt View, Microsoft Project
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Task bars with start/due dates visualization
  - ✅ Drag bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate in real-time)
  - ✅ Resize bars to change duration
  - ✅ 3 zoom levels (day/week/month) with responsive scale
  - ✅ Today marker with indicator dot and label
  - ✅ Swimlanes by task status (backlog/todo/inprogress/review/done) - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority-based color coding
  - ✅ Dependency arrows (SimpleTaskDependency with SVG overlay)
  - ✅ Row-based positioning for clarity
  - ✅ Responsive scrolling and empty state handling
  - ✅ Tab interface in Tasks page (Board/List/Timeline toggle)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - When parent task moves, children don't auto-adjust
     - Monday.com: ✅ "Shift all dependent tasks by however many days needed"
     - Asana: ✅ "Auto-scheduling" - blocked tasks automatically adjust dates
     - MS Project: ✅ Standard behavior, cascading date updates
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling required defeats purpose of dependencies - baseline in ALL competitors
  2. ❌ **Critical Path Analysis** - Cannot identify project bottlenecks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Critical path visualization with highlighting
     - Asana: ✅ Premium+ feature, shows longest sequence
     - MS Project: ✅ Core feature, red highlighting
     - **Impact:** Can't optimize project timeline or identify delays - professional PM baseline
  3. ❌ **Baseline Comparison** - Cannot track planned vs actual schedule
     - Monday.com: ✅ Baseline snapshot, green/red variance indicators
     - MS Project: ✅ Up to 10 baselines with variance tracking
     - **Impact:** No way to measure schedule variance or slippage - project management necessity
  4. ❌ **Progress Tracking (% Complete)** - No visual completion indicators on bars
     - Monday.com: ✅ Shows progress percentage within bars
     - Asana: ✅ Task percentage of completion display
     - MS Project: ✅ Progress bars within Gantt bars (filled portion)
     - **Impact:** Can't see task progress at a glance - baseline feature
  5. ❌ **Milestone Markers** - No way to mark key deadlines on timeline
     - Monday.com: ✅ Milestone tracking with diamond markers
     - Asana: ✅ Milestones as specialized tasks
     - MS Project: ✅ Diamond milestone markers on timeline
     - **Impact:** Can't highlight critical dates/deliverables - essential for project planning
  6. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only simple blocks/blocked-by
     - MS Project: ✅ 4 dependency types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag time
     - **Note:** Type definitions EXIST in `src/types/index.ts` (TaskDependency interface with DependencyType enum) but only SimpleTaskDependency is implemented
     - **Impact:** Can't model complex project relationships (parallel tasks, delayed starts)
  7. ❌ **Subitem Support on Timeline** - Subtasks don't appear on Gantt
     - Monday.com: ✅ Subitems visible on timeline
     - MS Project: ✅ Summary tasks with subtask rollup
     - **Impact:** Can't see task breakdown hierarchy visually
  8. ❌ **Export Timeline** - Cannot export Gantt to PDF/Excel/image
     - Monday.com: ✅ Export to Excel
     - MS Project: ✅ Export to PDF, Excel, images
     - **Impact:** Can't share timeline with stakeholders outside app
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Swimlanes by status (clearer organization than Asana)
  - ✅ Integrated with Kanban in single page (seamless workflow)
  - ✅ Free (Asana Gantt is Premium+ only)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Lightweight (no heavy dependencies)
- **Files:** `src/components/GanttView/GanttTimeline.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TimelineScale.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/TaskBar.tsx`, `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Auto-shift, Critical Path, Baseline), **P1** (Progress %, Milestones, Professional Dependencies), **P2** (Export, Subitem support)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Gantt with unique swimlane visualization, but missing professional project management capabilities
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt Chart](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, Asana, ClickUp
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns (daily, weekly, monthly, yearly) with intervals
  - ✅ Weekly: Days of week selection (M/T/W/Th/F/Sa/Su checkboxes)
  - ✅ Monthly: Day of month specification
  - ✅ End conditions: Never / After N occurrences / Until specific date
  - ✅ Auto-generation of next instance when current is completed
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation and display
  - ✅ Parent-instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component with all options
  - ✅ Integration with Kanban CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Instance management (view all instances of recurring task)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Only recurs from original due date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every day" (from due) vs "every! day" (from completion)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur from due date" vs "Recur from completion date" toggle
     - Asana: ✅ Same behavior with toggle
     - **Impact:** Flexible tasks (e.g., "call client every 5 days") can't be modeled - major use case gap
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every 2 weeks on Monday" parser
     - Todoist: ✅ Smart parser ("every weekday", "every 2 weeks", "every month on the 15th")
     - ClickUp: ✅ Natural language + visual picker hybrid
     - **Impact:** Slower to set up, requires clicking through UI - UX friction
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't pre-fill checklists/description
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use templates (checklists, custom fields, etc.)
     - Asana: ✅ Task templates can be recurring
     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup work for complex recurring tasks (e.g., weekly report with same checklist)
  4. ⚠️ **Advanced Patterns** - No "last Friday of month" or "2nd Tuesday"
     - Todoist: ✅ "every last friday" pattern
     - **Impact:** Some business patterns can't be expressed (e.g., monthly all-hands meeting on first Monday)
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Full UI integration (not hidden in advanced settings)
  - ✅ Instance relationship tracking (can see all occurrences)
  - ✅ Free (Todoist advanced recurrence requires Premium)
  - ✅ Offline-first
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 362-424)
- **Priority:** **P1** (Recur from completion - high user value), **P2** (Natural language, Template integration), **P3** (Advanced patterns)
- **Verdict:** Solid recurrence engine with all core patterns, but missing flexibility for real-world use cases
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://todoist.com/help/articles/set-a-recurring-due-date-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6310449699351-Recurring-Tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://help.asana.com/s/article/recurring-tasks?language=en_US)

🔶 **Task Dependencies** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Asana, ClickUp, Monday.com
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ SimpleTaskDependency: Basic blocks/blocked-by relationships
  - ✅ UI in CardDetailPanel (lines 615-750): Add blocker/blocked tasks with dropdowns
  - ✅ Visual indicators: ⚠️ Blocked By (yellow), 🔒 Blocks (blue accent)
  - ✅ Click dependency to navigate to related task
  - ✅ Remove dependencies with × button on hover
  - ✅ Circular dependency prevention in addDependency()
  - ✅ Self-dependency prevention
  - ✅ Validation: Can't complete task with incomplete blockers (useKanbanStore.ts:274-287)
  - ✅ Activity log tracking for dependency changes
  - ✅ Gantt timeline visualization (DependencyLine.tsx with SVG arrows)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Professional Dependency Types** - Only Finish-to-Start implemented
     - **IMPORTANT:** Full TaskDependency interface DEFINED in `src/types/index.ts` but NOT USED:
       ```typescript
       export type DependencyType = 'finish-to-start' | 'start-to-start' | 'finish-to-finish' | 'start-to-finish';
       export interface TaskDependency {
         taskId: string;
         type: DependencyType;
         lag: number; // Lag time in days (can be negative for lead)
       }
       ```
     - Asana: ✅ Basic dependencies only (similar to our current)
     - ClickUp: ✅ 4 types + waiting on/blocking
     - MS Project: ✅ All 4 types (FS/SS/FF/SF) + lag/lead time
     - **Impact:** Can't model parallel work (SS), coordinated finishes (FF), or complex schedules - professional PM baseline
  2. ❌ **Auto-shift Dependent Tasks** - Date changes don't cascade (already noted in Gantt audit)
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Rescheduling dependencies" option
     - MS Project: ✅ Automatic cascading by default
     - **Impact:** Manual rescheduling burden, defeats dependency purpose
  3. ❌ **Lag/Lead Time** - No "+3 days after" or "-2 days before"
     - MS Project: ✅ Lag time in dependency definition
     - **Impact:** Can't express "start QA 2 days before dev finishes" - real-world scheduling need
  4. ❌ **Dependency Warnings** - No alerts when blocker is overdue
     - ClickUp: ✅ Warning badges when blocked by overdue tasks
     - Monday.com: ✅ Dependency status indicators
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, tasks stuck without visibility
- **Quick Win Opportunity:** TaskDependency types already defined, just need to:
  1. Add type selector to CardDetailPanel dependency UI
  2. Update addDependency() to accept type + lag parameters
  3. Implement cascading date logic in GanttTimeline
  4. Add lag time input to dependency creation
- **Files:** `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (lines 1019-1105), `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx` (lines 615-750), `src/types/index.ts` (lines 53-63, 98-103), `src/components/GanttView/DependencyLine.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Professional types + lag - types already defined!), **P1** (Auto-shift, Warnings)
- **Verdict:** Basic dependency tracking works, but professional PM features are defined but unimplemented - low-hanging fruit
- **Sources:** [Asana Dependencies](https://help.asana.com/s/article/dependencies?language=en_US), [ClickUp Task Dependencies](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305955166359-Task-Dependencies), [MS Project Dependencies](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/link-tasks-in-a-project-d7c17100-6dc7-49b6-b5f1-30ed86dbd079)

🔶 **Custom Fields** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)

### Notes & Knowledge Management
🔶 **Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ Wiki-style link syntax: `[[Note Title]]` (LINK_PATTERN regex in backlinks.ts)
  - ✅ Link extraction from note content (`extractWikiLinks()`)
  - ✅ Link resolution: Exact + partial title matching (`resolveLinksToIds()`)
  - ✅ Backlinks panel component (BacklinksPanel.tsx) with count badge
  - ✅ Click backlink to navigate to source note
  - ✅ Empty state: "Use [[Note Title]] to create links" instruction
  - ✅ Auto-update linked notes when content changes (`updateLinkedNotes()`)
  - ✅ linkedNotes array stored on Note type
  - ✅ Backlinks panel integrated in NotesEditor
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Unlinked Mentions** - No detection of note titles mentioned without brackets
     - Obsidian: ✅ "Unlinked mentions" section showing implicit references
     - Roam: ✅ Detects unlinked references automatically
     - Logseq: ✅ Shows both linked and unlinked references
     - **Impact:** Miss implicit connections, manual linking burden
  2. ❌ **Link Autocomplete** - No `[[` trigger for note picker
     - Obsidian: ✅ Type `[[` to open note search popup
     - Roam: ✅ Instant search on `[[` with fuzzy matching
     - Logseq: ✅ Page autocomplete on `[[`
     - **Impact:** Must remember exact titles, typos create broken links
  3. ❌ **Broken Link Detection** - No warnings for links to non-existent notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Purple color for broken links, "Create note" option
     - Roam: ✅ Auto-creates page on click
     - Logseq: ✅ Broken link styling + creation option
     - **Impact:** Silent failures, unclear if link is valid
  4. ❌ **Block-level Links** - Can't link to specific paragraphs/headings
     - Obsidian: ✅ `[[Note#Heading]]` and `[[Note^block-id]]` syntax
     - Roam: ✅ Block references with `((block-id))`
     - Logseq: ✅ Block embedding and references
     - **Impact:** Must link to entire note, not specific sections
  5. ❌ **Link Preview on Hover** - No popover showing linked note content
     - Obsidian: ✅ Hover preview with note excerpt
     - Roam: ✅ Inline preview on hover
     - **Impact:** Must click links to see content, slows research flow
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Partial matching (finds "JavaScript Basics" when linking "JavaScript")
  - ✅ Clean UI with count badges
  - ✅ Offline-first
- **Files:** `src/utils/backlinks.ts`, `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (line 79), `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Link autocomplete - UX blocker), **P1** (Unlinked mentions, Broken link detection), **P2** (Block links, Hover preview)
- **Verdict:** Basic bidirectional linking works, but missing PKM essentials for serious knowledge work
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Linking](https://help.obsidian.md/Linking+notes+and+files/Internal+links), [Roam Research Bidirectional Links](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/Roam_Research_White_Paper), [Logseq Backlinks](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/contents)

     - Logseq: ✅ Local graph with depth slider
     - **Impact:** Can't focus on specific note's neighborhood, overwhelming for large graphs
  2. ❌ **Filters** - No ability to filter by tags, folders, date ranges
     - Obsidian: ✅ Filter by tags, folders, paths, search terms
     - Logseq: ✅ Filter by page properties, tags, namespaces
     - **Impact:** Graph becomes unusable with 100+ notes, no way to focus
  3. ❌ **Node Sizing** - All nodes same size, no indication of importance
     - Obsidian: ✅ Node size = number of connections (degree centrality)
     - Roam: ✅ Node size based on reference count
     - **Impact:** Can't identify hub notes or important concepts visually
  4. ❌ **Link Strength** - No indication of connection frequency
     - Obsidian: ✅ Link thickness = number of references
     - **Impact:** All connections appear equal weight
  5. ❌ **Color Groups** - No automatic clustering or coloring by category
     - Obsidian: ✅ Color groups by tags, folders, or custom groups
     - Logseq: ✅ Namespace-based coloring
     - **Impact:** Hard to see thematic clusters in knowledge base
  6. ❌ **Orphan Detection** - No highlighting of isolated notes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Shows orphan notes (no connections)
     - **Impact:** Can't identify disconnected knowledge
  7. ❌ **Search in Graph** - No search to highlight specific nodes
     - Obsidian: ✅ Search bar highlights matching nodes
     - **Impact:** Must visually scan to find notes
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Tag relationships visualized (not just note-to-note)
  - ✅ Smooth physics simulation
  - ✅ Free (comparable to Obsidian which is also free for local graphs)
- **Files:** `src/components/Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx`, `src/utils/graphSimulation.ts`, `src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts`, `src/pages/GraphView.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Local graph, Filters - unusable without these at scale), **P1** (Node sizing, Color groups), **P2** (Link strength, Search, Orphans)
- **Verdict:** Beautiful graph visualization, but impractical for actual knowledge management at scale
- **Sources:** [Obsidian Graph View](https://help.obsidian.md/Plugins/Graph+view), [Logseq Graph](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/graph%20view), [Roam Graph](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/graph-view)

🔶 **Notes - Tags System** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Bear, Notion, Obsidian
- **Current Implementation:**
  - ✅ Array of string tags on Note type (`tags: string[]`)
  - ✅ TagPicker component with autocomplete (TagPicker.tsx)
  - ✅ Tag filtering in Notes page
  - ✅ Tag display on note cards
  - ✅ Free-text tag creation
  - ✅ Tags integrated with Graph View (tag nodes + relationships)
  - ✅ Daily notes auto-tags: `daily-note`, `{year}`, `month-{MM}`
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
     - Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator

   291→- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://todoist.com/help/articles/set-a-recurring-due-date-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6310449699351-Recurring-Tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://help.asana.com/s/article/recurring-tasks?language=en_US)

   339→- **Priority:** **P0** (Professional types + lag - types already defined!), **P1** (Auto-shift, Warnings)
   340→- **Verdict:** Basic dependency tracking works, but professional PM features are defined but unimplemented - low-hanging fruit
   341→- **Sources:** [Asana Dependencies](https://help.asana.com/s/article/dependencies?language=en_US), [ClickUp Task Dependencies](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305955166359-Task-Dependencies), [MS Project Dependencies](https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/link-tasks-in-a-project-d7c17100-6dc7-49b6-b5f1-30ed86dbd079)
   342→
   343→🔶 **Custom Fields** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   344→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   345→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Monday.com, ClickUp, Airtable
   346→- **Current Implementation:**
   347→  - ✅ 5 field types: text, number, date, select, checkbox (src/types/customFields.ts)
   348→  - ✅ FieldDefinition schema: id, name, type, defaultValue, options, required, description, timestamps
   349→  - ✅ Field validation with validateFieldValue() function
   350→  - ✅ CustomFieldEditor component for rendering appropriate inputs (src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx)
   351→  - ✅ Accessibility: ARIA labels, required indicators, error messaging
   352→  - ✅ Semantic styling with Tailwind tokens
   353→  - ✅ Integration with useSettingsStore for field definitions
   354→  - ✅ CustomFieldsMap type for storing values on tasks/notes
   355→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   356→  1. ❌ **Field Management UI** - No interface to create/edit/delete field definitions
   357→     - Monday.com: ✅ Column center for adding/managing custom columns
   358→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom fields manager with templates
   359→     - Airtable: ✅ Field type selector with full customization
   360→     - **Impact:** Users can't actually USE the custom fields system - implementation is backend-only
   361→  2. ❌ **Additional Field Types** - Missing common types
   362→     - Monday.com: ✅ Also has: Person, Status, Timeline, Rating, File, Link, Formula
   363→     - ClickUp: ✅ Also has: Dropdown (multi-select), Progress, Labels, Relationships, Email, Phone, URL
   364→     - Airtable: ✅ Also has: Multi-select, Long text, Attachment, URL, Email, Phone, Rating, Barcode, Formula, Rollup, Lookup
   365→     - **Impact:** Can't model real-world data (file uploads, user assignment, calculations)
   366→  3. ❌ **Field Display in Lists** - Custom fields not shown in Kanban card list/board views
   367→     - Monday.com: ✅ Custom columns visible in board view
   368→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom fields show in list/board with toggles
   369→     - **Impact:** Fields are hidden unless you open card detail - low visibility
   370→  4. ❌ **Field Templates** - No reusable field sets
   371→     - ClickUp: ✅ Custom field templates (e.g., "Bug Report" field set)
   372→     - **Impact:** Repetitive setup for similar task types
   373→  5. ❌ **Formulas & Rollups** - No computed fields
   374→     - Airtable: ✅ Formula fields, rollup from linked records
   375→     - Monday.com: ✅ Formula columns
   376→     - **Impact:** Can't auto-calculate totals, averages, or derive values
   377→- **Our Foundation Strengths:**
   378→  - ✅ Type-safe field definitions with Zod validation
   379→  - ✅ Reusable CustomFieldEditor component
   380→  - ✅ Accessible UI patterns established
   381→  - ✅ Clean separation of concerns (types, validation, rendering)
   382→- **Files:** `src/types/customFields.ts`, `src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx`, `src/components/CustomFieldsSettings.tsx`, `src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts`
   383→- **Priority:** **P0** (Field Management UI - without it, feature is unusable), **P1** (Display in views, Additional types), **P2** (Templates, Formulas)
   384→- **Verdict:** Well-architected foundation with NO user-facing interface - feature is 90% invisible
   385→- **Sources:** [Monday.com Custom Columns](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/115005348365-Custom-Columns), [ClickUp Custom Fields](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6305790296087-Custom-Fields), [Airtable Field Types](https://www.airtable.com/guides/start/field-types)
   386→
   387→### Notes & Knowledge Management
   388→🔶 **Notes - Backlinks/Wikilinks** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   389→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   390→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
   391→- **Current Implementation:**
   392→  - ✅ Wiki-style link syntax: `[[Note Title]]` (LINK_PATTERN regex in backlinks.ts)
   393→  - ✅ Link extraction from note content (`extractWikiLinks()`)
   394→  - ✅ Link resolution: Exact + partial title matching (`resolveLinksToIds()`)
   395→  - ✅ Backlinks panel component (BacklinksPanel.tsx) with count badge
   396→  - ✅ Click backlink to navigate to source note
   397→  - ✅ Empty state: "Use [[Note Title]] to create links" instruction
   398→  - ✅ Auto-update linked notes when content changes (`updateLinkedNotes()`)
   399→  - ✅ linkedNotes array stored on Note type
   400→  - ✅ Backlinks panel integrated in NotesEditor
   401→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   402→  1. ❌ **Unlinked Mentions** - No detection of note titles mentioned without brackets
   403→     - Obsidian: ✅ "Unlinked mentions" section showing implicit references
   404→     - Roam: ✅ Detects unlinked references automatically
   405→     - Logseq: ✅ Shows both linked and unlinked references
   406→     - **Impact:** Miss implicit connections, manual linking burden
   407→  2. ❌ **Link Autocomplete** - No `[[` trigger for note picker
   408→     - Obsidian: ✅ Type `[[` to open note search popup
   409→     - Roam: ✅ Instant search on `[[` with fuzzy matching
   410→     - Logseq: ✅ Page autocomplete on `[[`
   411→     - **Impact:** Must remember exact titles, typos create broken links
   412→  3. ❌ **Broken Link Detection** - No warnings for links to non-existent notes
   413→     - Obsidian: ✅ Purple color for broken links, "Create note" option
   414→     - Roam: ✅ Auto-creates page on click
   415→     - Logseq: ✅ Broken link styling + creation option
   416→     - **Impact:** Silent failures, unclear if link is valid
   417→  4. ❌ **Block-level Links** - Can't link to specific paragraphs/headings
   418→     - Obsidian: ✅ `[[Note#Heading]]` and `[[Note^block-id]]` syntax
   419→     - Roam: ✅ Block references with `((block-id))`
   420→     - Logseq: ✅ Block embedding and references
   421→     - **Impact:** Must link to entire note, not specific sections
   422→  5. ❌ **Link Preview on Hover** - No popover showing linked note content
   423→     - Obsidian: ✅ Hover preview with note excerpt
   424→     - Roam: ✅ Inline preview on hover
   425→     - **Impact:** Must click links to see content, slows research flow
   426→- **Our Advantages:**
   427→  - ✅ Partial matching (finds "JavaScript Basics" when linking "JavaScript")
   428→  - ✅ Clean UI with count badges
   429→  - ✅ Offline-first
   430→- **Files:** `src/utils/backlinks.ts`, `src/components/BacklinksPanel.tsx`, `src/widgets/NotesEditor.tsx` (line 79), `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
   431→- **Priority:** **P0** (Link autocomplete - UX blocker), **P1** (Unlinked mentions, Broken link detection), **P2** (Block links, Hover preview)
   432→- **Verdict:** Basic bidirectional linking works, but missing PKM essentials for serious knowledge work
   433→- **Sources:** [Obsidian Linking](https://help.obsidian.md/Linking+notes+and+files/Internal+links), [Roam Research Bidirectional Links](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/Roam_Research_White_Paper), [Logseq Backlinks](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/contents)
   434→
   435→🔶 **Notes - Graph View** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   436→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   437→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Obsidian, Roam Research, Logseq
   438→- **Current Implementation:**
   439→  - ✅ D3 force-directed graph (GraphCanvas.tsx using d3.forceSimulation)
   440→  - ✅ Node types: Notes (circles) and Tags (differentiated)
   441→  - ✅ Edge types: Backlinks (cyan arrows), Tag relationships
   442→  - ✅ Zoom/pan with d3.zoom (scale 0.1-4x)
   443→  - ✅ Drag nodes to reposition (with physics simulation)
   444→  - ✅ Click node to navigate (onNodeClick callback)
   445→  - ✅ Physics forces: collision, charge (repulsion), centering, link distance
   446→  - ✅ Arrow markers for directed edges
   447→  - ✅ Color coding: backlinks (cyan), regular edges (border color)
   448→  - ✅ SVG-based rendering for performance
   449→  - ✅ Graph data processing utilities (graphDataProcessor.ts)
   450→  - ✅ Dedicated /graph-view page
   451→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   452→  1. ❌ **Local Graph** - No focused view for single note's connections
   453→     - Obsidian: ✅ Local graph showing 1-2 hop neighbors
   454→     - Roam: ✅ Page graph for current page
   455→     - Logseq: ✅ Local graph with depth slider
   456→     - **Impact:** Can't focus on specific note's neighborhood, overwhelming for large graphs
   457→  2. ❌ **Filters** - No ability to filter by tags, folders, date ranges
   458→     - Obsidian: ✅ Filter by tags, folders, paths, search terms
   459→     - Logseq: ✅ Filter by page properties, tags, namespaces
   460→     - **Impact:** Graph becomes unusable with 100+ notes, no way to focus
   461→  3. ❌ **Node Sizing** - All nodes same size, no indication of importance
   462→     - Obsidian: ✅ Node size = number of connections (degree centrality)
   463→     - Roam: ✅ Node size based on reference count
   464→     - **Impact:** Can't identify hub notes or important concepts visually
   465→  4. ❌ **Link Strength** - No indication of connection frequency
   466→     - Obsidian: ✅ Link thickness = number of references
   467→     - **Impact:** All connections appear equal weight
   468→  5. ❌ **Color Groups** - No automatic clustering or coloring by category
   469→     - Obsidian: ✅ Color groups by tags, folders, or custom groups
   470→     - Logseq: ✅ Namespace-based coloring
   471→     - **Impact:** Hard to see thematic clusters in knowledge base
   472→  6. ❌ **Orphan Detection** - No highlighting of isolated notes
   473→     - Obsidian: ✅ Shows orphan notes (no connections)
   474→     - **Impact:** Can't identify disconnected knowledge
   475→  7. ❌ **Search in Graph** - No search to highlight specific nodes
   476→     - Obsidian: ✅ Search bar highlights matching nodes
   477→     - **Impact:** Must visually scan to find notes
   478→- **Our Advantages:**
   479→  - ✅ Tag relationships visualized (not just note-to-note)
   480→  - ✅ Smooth physics simulation
   481→  - ✅ Free (comparable to Obsidian which is also free for local graphs)
   482→- **Files:** `src/components/Graph/GraphCanvas.tsx`, `src/utils/graphSimulation.ts`, `src/utils/graphDataProcessor.ts`, `src/pages/GraphView.tsx`
   483→- **Priority:** **P0** (Local graph, Filters - unusable without these at scale), **P1** (Node sizing, Color groups), **P2** (Link strength, Search, Orphans)
   484→- **Verdict:** Beautiful graph visualization, but impractical for actual knowledge management at scale
   485→- **Sources:** [Obsidian Graph View](https://help.obsidian.md/Plugins/Graph+view), [Logseq Graph](https://docs.logseq.com/#/page/graph%20view), [Roam Graph](https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/graph-view)
   486→
   487→🔶 **Notes - Tags System** (Status: PARTIAL - Audited 2025-11-28)
   488→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   489→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Bear, Notion, Obsidian
   490→- **Current Implementation:**
   491→  - ✅ Array of string tags on Note type (`tags: string[]`)
   492→  - ✅ TagPicker component with autocomplete (TagPicker.tsx)
   493→  - ✅ Tag filtering in Notes page
   494→  - ✅ Tag display on note cards
   495→  - ✅ Free-text tag creation
   496→  - ✅ Tags integrated with Graph View (tag nodes + relationships)
   497→  - ✅ Daily notes auto-tags: `daily-note`, `{year}`, `month-{MM}`
   498→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   499→  1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
