1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
     - Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator
     - Obsidian: ✅ Nested tags with `#parent/child` syntax
     - Notion: ✅ Tag groups with parent-child relationships
     - **Impact:** Flat tag namespace becomes messy with 50+ tags
  2. ❌ **Tag Manager** - No centralized tag management UI
     - Bear: ✅ Sidebar shows all tags with count and hierarchy
     - Notion: ✅ Tag database with rename/merge/delete
     - Obsidian: ✅ Tag pane with usage counts
     - **Impact:** Can't rename tags globally, typos create duplicates
  3. ❌ **Tag Colors** - No custom colors for visual organization
     - Bear: ✅ Custom tag colors/icons
     - Notion: ✅ Color-coded tags
     - **Impact:** All tags look the same, harder to scan
  4. ❌ **Tag Suggestions** - No smart tag recommendations based on content
     - Notion: ✅ AI-powered tag suggestions
     - **Impact:** Manual tagging burden, inconsistent taxonomy
  5. ❌ **Multi-tag Operations** - Can't bulk add/remove tags
     - Bear: ✅ Select multiple notes → add/remove tags
     - Notion: ✅ Bulk property editing
     - **Impact:** Tedious to reorganize or reclassify notes
  6. ❌ **Inline Tags** - Tags only in metadata, not in note body
     - Obsidian: ✅ `#tag` anywhere in note content
     - Roam: ✅ `#tag` and `#[[multi-word tag]]` inline
     - **Impact:** Must use separate UI, interrupts writing flow
- **Our Implementation:**
  - ✅ Works with autocomplete
  - ✅ Graph integration (unique)
  - ✅ Auto-tags for daily notes (helpful)
- **Files:** `src/types/notes.ts`, `src/components/TagPicker.tsx`, `src/components/TagFilter.tsx`, `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

### AI Terminal

- **Current:** Shows "not supported" for native commands
- **Missing:** Web-compatible alternatives
- **Needed Commands:**
  - `/ping` - fetch-based latency test
  - `/curl` - fetch wrapper
  - `/git` - isomorphic-git integration
  - `/python` - Pyodide integration (heavy)
  - `/docker` - N/A (cannot emulate)

- **Implementation:** IndexedDB encrypted storage

### Voice & Audio

- **API:** Web Speech API (browser-native)
- **Privacy:** On-device processing
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Voice Commands**
- **Examples:** "Create task...", "Open notes"
- **Priority:** LOW

   529→- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
   530→- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
   531→- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
   532→- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features

   534→
   535→🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
   536→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   537→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
   538→- **Current Features:**
   539→  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
   540→  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
   541→  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
   542→  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
   543→  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
   544→  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
   545→  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
   546→  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
   547→  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
   548→  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
   549→  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
   550→  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
   551→  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
   552→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   553→  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
   554→     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
   555→     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
   556→     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
   557→     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
   558→     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
   559→  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
   560→     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
   561→     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
   562→     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
   563→     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
   564→  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
   565→     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
   566→     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
   567→     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
   568→     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
   569→  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
   570→     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
   571→     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
   572→     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
   573→- **Our Unique Advantages:**
   574→  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
   575→  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
   576→  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
   577→  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
   578→- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
   579→- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
   580→- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
   581→- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)
   582→

file.content:
- Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator
     - Obsidian: ✅ Nested tags with `#parent/child` syntax
     - Notion: ✅ Tag groups with parent-child relationships
     - **Impact:** Flat tag namespace becomes messy with 50+ tags
  2. ❌ **Tag Manager** - No centralized tag management UI
     - Bear: ✅ Sidebar shows all tags with count and hierarchy
     - Notion: ✅ Tag database with rename/merge/delete
     - Obsidian: ✅ Tag pane with usage counts
     - **Impact:** Can't rename tags globally, typos create duplicates
  3. ❌ **Tag Colors** - No custom colors for visual organization
     - Bear: ✅ Custom tag colors/icons
     - Notion: ✅ Color-coded tags
     - **Impact:** All tags look the same, harder to scan
  4. ❌ **Tag Suggestions** - No smart tag recommendations based on content
     - Notion: ✅ AI-powered tag suggestions
     - **Impact:** Manual tagging burden, inconsistent taxonomy
  5. ❌ **Multi-tag Operations** - Can't bulk add/remove tags
     - Bear: ✅ Select multiple notes → add/remove tags
     - Notion: ✅ Bulk property editing
     - **Impact:** Tedious to reorganize or reclassify notes
  6. ❌ **Inline Tags** - Tags only in metadata, not in note body
     - Obsidian: ✅ `#tag` anywhere in note content
     - Roam: ✅ `#tag` and `#[[multi-word tag]]` inline
     - **Impact:** Must use separate UI, interrupts writing flow
- **Our Implementation:**
  - ✅ Works with autocomplete
  - ✅ Graph integration (unique)
  - ✅ Auto-tags for daily notes (helpful)
- **Files:** `src/types/notes.ts`, `src/components/TagPicker.tsx`, `src/components/TagFilter.tsx`, `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

- **Current:** Shows upcoming events
- **Missing:** Configuration for "how far out" or "how many events"
- **Recommendation:** Show next 5 events regardless of date
- **File:** src/widgets/Dashboard/UpcomingEventsWidget.tsx

- **Missing:** Configurable timeframe filter

- **Recommendation:** Make timeframe configurable

### Notes System

- **Missing:** Many modern note-taking features
- **Research Needed:** See [Research Needed](#research-needed) section

### Forms & Data Collection
✅ **Form Builder** (Status: COMPLETED - Implemented 2025-11-27)
- Full-screen form builder with field editor and live preview
- 11 field types: text, textarea, number, date, time, select, multiselect, radio, checkbox, rating, scale
- Field validation (required, min/max for numbers)
- Field reordering with up/down buttons
- Field duplication and deletion
- Form settings: title, description, allowMultipleSubmissions
✅ **Form Renderer** (Status: COMPLETED - Implemented 2025-11-27)
- Dynamic form rendering from templates
- Client-side validation with error messages
- Scroll to first error on submit
- Success state after submission
✅ **Form Responses** (Status: COMPLETED - Implemented 2025-11-27)
- View all responses with submission timestamps
- Delete individual responses or clear all
- CSV export with proper field type formatting
- Response count and recent activity stats
✅ **Forms Dashboard Widget** (Status: COMPLETED - Implemented 2025-11-27)
- Total forms, responses, active forms, recent forms stats
- Top 5 forms by response count
- Quick navigation to form responses
- New form button

### AI Terminal
🔶 **Phantom Shell Web Alternatives** (Status: PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Shows "not supported" for native commands
- **Missing:** Web-compatible alternatives
- **Needed Commands:**
  - `/ping` - fetch-based latency test
  - `/curl` - fetch wrapper
  - `/git` - isomorphic-git integration
  - `/python` - Pyodide integration (heavy)
  - `/docker` - N/A (cannot emulate)
  - `/ssh` - N/A (cannot emulate)

### Link Library
🔶 **Browser Bookmark Sync** (Status: RESEARCH NEEDED → PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Manual export/import
- **Desired:** Direct browser API integration
- **See:** Browser Integration section

   562→     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
   563→     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
   564→  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
   565→     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
   566→     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
   567→     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
   568→     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
   569→  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
   570→     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
   571→     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
   572→     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
   573→- **Our Unique Advantages:**
   574→  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
   575→  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
   576→  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)

   578→- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
   579→- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)

file.content:
- Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator
     - Obsidian: ✅ Nested tags with `#parent/child` syntax
     - Notion: ✅ Tag groups with parent-child relationships
     - **Impact:** Flat tag namespace becomes messy with 50+ tags
  2. ❌ **Tag Manager** - No centralized tag management UI
     - Bear: ✅ Sidebar shows all tags with count and hierarchy
     - Notion: ✅ Tag database with rename/merge/delete
     - Obsidian: ✅ Tag pane with usage counts
     - **Impact:** Can't rename tags globally, typos create duplicates
  3. ❌ **Tag Colors** - No custom colors for visual organization
     - Bear: ✅ Custom tag colors/icons
     - Notion: ✅ Color-coded tags
     - **Impact:** All tags look the same, harder to scan
  4. ❌ **Tag Suggestions** - No smart tag recommendations based on content
     - Notion: ✅ AI-powered tag suggestions
     - **Impact:** Manual tagging burden, inconsistent taxonomy
  5. ❌ **Multi-tag Operations** - Can't bulk add/remove tags
     - Bear: ✅ Select multiple notes → add/remove tags
     - Notion: ✅ Bulk property editing
     - **Impact:** Tedious to reorganize or reclassify notes
  6. ❌ **Inline Tags** - Tags only in metadata, not in note body
     - Obsidian: ✅ `#tag` anywhere in note content
     - Roam: ✅ `#tag` and `#[[multi-word tag]]` inline
     - **Impact:** Must use separate UI, interrupts writing flow
- **Our Implementation:**
  - ✅ Works with autocomplete
  - ✅ Graph integration (unique)
  - ✅ Auto-tags for daily notes (helpful)
- **Files:** `src/types/notes.ts`, `src/components/TagPicker.tsx`, `src/components/TagFilter.tsx`, `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level Timeline with good basic visualization, but missing ALL professional project management features
- **Sources:** [Monday.com Gantt](https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360015643840-The-Gantt-Chart-View-and-Widget), [Asana Gantt](https://help.asana.com/s/article/gantt-view?language=en_US), [MS Project Gantt](https://www.projectmanager.com/blog/microsoft-project-gantt-chart)

🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

   539→  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)

   541→  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date

   550→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   551→  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
   552→     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
   553→     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
   554→     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
   555→     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
   556→     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
   557→  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
   558→     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
   559→     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
   560→     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
   561→     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
   562→  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
   563→     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
   564→     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
   565→     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
   566→     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
   567→  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
   568→     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
   569→     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
   570→     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
   571→- **Our Unique Advantages:**
   572→  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
   573→  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
   574→  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
   575→  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
   576→- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)

   578→- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect

   645→     - **Impact:** Can't track marketing campaigns via forms

   647→     - Google Forms: ✅ Quiz mode with auto-scoring
   648→     - Typeform: ✅ Quiz mode (paid)
   649→     - Tally: ✅ Quiz features
   650→     - **Impact:** Can't create assessments or knowledge tests
   651→  9. ❌ **Custom Thank You Page** - No redirect after submission
   652→     - Google Forms: ✅ Custom confirmation message
   653→     - Typeform: ✅ Custom thank you page
   654→     - Tally: ✅ Redirect after submission
   655→     - **Impact:** Can't guide users to next action
   656→- **Our Advantages:**
   657→  - ✅ FREE (Typeform charges for logic, scoring, hidden fields)
   658→  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
   659→  - ✅ Clean, minimal UI
   660→  - ✅ Well-typed TypeScript implementation
   661→  - ✅ No data on external servers (privacy)
   662→- **Files:** `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx`, `src/components/FormRenderer/FormRenderer.tsx`, `src/types/forms.ts`, `src/stores/useFormsStore.ts`
   663→- **Priority:** **P0** (Conditional logic, File upload, Progress bar, Analytics, Spam protection), **P1** (Answer piping, Hidden fields, Quiz mode)
   664→- **Verdict:** MVP-level form builder with core functionality, but missing advanced features users expect from modern form tools - Tally offers more features for FREE
   665→- **Sources:** [Typeform vs Tally](https://www.typeform.com/blog/typeform-vs-tally), [Google Forms Features](https://clickydrip.com/google-forms-features/), [Form Builder Must-Haves](https://www.thehumancapitalhub.com/articles/the-7-best-form-builder-tools-for-20252026)

   667→### Diagramming & Visual Tools
   668→🔶 **Diagram Editor** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
   669→- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
   670→- **Competitors Analyzed:** Excalidraw, Draw.io (diagrams.net), Lucidchart
   671→- **Current Features:**
   672→  - ✅ Konva-based canvas rendering (~50KB library)
   673→  - ✅ 11 shape types: rectangle, circle, ellipse, triangle, diamond, line, arrow, text, freehand, connector (straight/curved/orthogonal)
   674→  - ✅ Drag-to-move shapes with physics
   675→  - ✅ Resize/rotate shapes with transform handles

   677→  - ✅ Undo/Redo (50-step history)

   680→  - ✅ Z-index management (bring to front/send to back)

   682→  - ✅ Grid background with snap-to-grid (20px, configurable)

   684→  - ✅ Text formatting (fontSize, fontWeight, textAlign)

   689→  - ✅ Freehand drawing tool

   691→  - ✅ Full-screen editor at `/diagrams/:id`
   692→  - ✅ Library view with grid/list modes, search, create/delete
   693→- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
   694→  1. ❌ **Shape Libraries** - No pre-built shapes (flowchart, UML, network, AWS, etc.)
   695→     - Draw.io: ✅ 1000+ shapes across 50+ categories (flowcharts, UML, network diagrams, cloud architecture)
   696→     - Lucidchart: ✅ Extensive professional shape libraries
   697→     - Excalidraw: ✅ Built-in shape library

1. ❌ **Nested Tags** - No hierarchy like `projects/web` or `archive/2023`
     - Bear: ✅ Multi-level tag hierarchy with `/` separator
     - Obsidian: ✅ Nested tags with `#parent/child` syntax
     - Notion: ✅ Tag groups with parent-child relationships
     - **Impact:** Flat tag namespace becomes messy with 50+ tags
  2. ❌ **Tag Manager** - No centralized tag management UI
     - Bear: ✅ Sidebar shows all tags with count and hierarchy
     - Notion: ✅ Tag database with rename/merge/delete
     - Obsidian: ✅ Tag pane with usage counts
     - **Impact:** Can't rename tags globally, typos create duplicates
  3. ❌ **Tag Colors** - No custom colors for visual organization
     - Bear: ✅ Custom tag colors/icons
     - Notion: ✅ Color-coded tags
     - **Impact:** All tags look the same, harder to scan
  4. ❌ **Tag Suggestions** - No smart tag recommendations based on content
     - Notion: ✅ AI-powered tag suggestions
     - **Impact:** Manual tagging burden, inconsistent taxonomy
  5. ❌ **Multi-tag Operations** - Can't bulk add/remove tags
     - Bear: ✅ Select multiple notes → add/remove tags
     - Notion: ✅ Bulk property editing
     - **Impact:** Tedious to reorganize or reclassify notes
  6. ❌ **Inline Tags** - Tags only in metadata, not in note body
     - Obsidian: ✅ `#tag` anywhere in note content
     - Roam: ✅ `#tag` and `#[[multi-word tag]]` inline
     - **Impact:** Must use separate UI, interrupts writing flow
- **Our Implementation:**
  - ✅ Works with autocomplete
  - ✅ Graph integration (unique)
  - ✅ Auto-tags for daily notes (helpful)
- **Files:** `src/types/notes.ts`, `src/components/TagPicker.tsx`, `src/components/TagFilter.tsx`, `src/stores/useNotesStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Nested tags - essential for organization), **P1** (Tag Manager, Inline tags), **P2** (Colors, Suggestions, Bulk ops)
- **Verdict:** Basic tagging works, but missing organizational features for large note collections
- **Sources:** [Bear Tags](https://bear.app/faq/Tags%20&%20Linking/), [Notion Tags](https://www.notion.so/help/database-properties#select-and-multi-select), [Obsidian Tags](https://help.obsidian.md/Editing+and+formatting/Tags)
🔶 **Recurring Tasks** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Todoist, ClickUp, Asana
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Frequency patterns: daily/weekly/monthly/yearly
  - ✅ Custom intervals (every 2 weeks, every 3 months, etc.)
  - ✅ Days of week selection (for weekly recurrence)
  - ✅ Day of month selection (for monthly recurrence, 1-31)
  - ✅ End conditions: never/after N occurrences/until date
  - ✅ On-demand instance generation (not pre-generated)
  - ✅ Parent/instance relationship tracking (recurrenceId, isRecurringParent)
  - ✅ Next occurrence calculation
  - ✅ Instance deduplication (prevents duplicate instances)
  - ✅ Series end detection (respects endCount and endDate)
  - ✅ RecurrencePicker UI component integrated into CardDetailPanel
  - ✅ Visual recurrence editor (matches Calendar Events pattern)
  - ✅ Full RRULE-like pattern support
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Recur from Completion Date** - Tasks always recur from original date, not completion date
     - Todoist: ✅ "every" vs "every!" syntax - "every! 3 months" recurs 3 months from completion
     - Asana: ✅ "Periodically" option - repeats 1-30 days after task is completed
     - ClickUp: ✅ "Recur only after completion" checkbox
     - **Impact:** CRITICAL for maintenance tasks, habits, and periodic reviews where completion date matters
     - **Example:** "Change oil filter every 3 months" should be 3 months from LAST change, not original date
  2. ❌ **Natural Language Input** - No "every Monday" or "monthly" parsing
     - Todoist: ✅ Full natural language parsing ("every third Thursday", "monthly", "every 2 weeks on Friday")
     - ClickUp: Partial (date picker focused)
     - Asana: ❌ No natural language
     - **Impact:** Slower task creation, requires clicking through UI
  3. ❌ **Template Integration** - Recurring tasks don't use task templates
     - ClickUp: ✅ Recurring tasks can use task templates - each instance starts with same structure/checklists
     - Asana: ⚠️ Subtasks copy but not full templates
     - Todoist: ❌ No templates
     - **Impact:** Each recurring instance is identical copy - can't have dynamic checklists or varying structure
  4. ⚠️ **Recurrence Exceptions** - Can edit instances but no visual indication
     - Have `recurrenceException` field in types but not fully implemented in UI
     - No "edit just this instance" vs "edit all future instances" workflow
     - **Impact:** Confusing UX when instances are modified
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ On-demand generation (doesn't clutter task list with future instances)
  - ✅ Matches Calendar Events pattern (consistent UX)
  - ✅ Offline-first (works without internet)
  - ✅ Clean parent/instance relationship model
- **Files:** `src/services/taskRecurrence.ts` (lines 1-201), `src/utils/recurrence.ts`, `src/components/RecurrencePicker.tsx`, `src/types/index.ts` (lines 209-221)
- **Priority:** **P0** (Recur from completion), **P1** (Natural language, Template integration), **P2** (Exception handling UI)
- **Verdict:** Strong recurrence engine with professional patterns, but missing key flexibility (completion-based recurrence) that users expect
- **Sources:** [Todoist Recurring Tasks](https://www.todoist.com/help/articles/introduction-to-recurring-dates-YUYVJJAV), [ClickUp Recurring Tasks](https://help.clickup.com/hc/en-us/articles/6309885016471-Use-recurring-tasks), [Asana Recurring Tasks](https://blog.coupler.io/asana-recurring-tasks/)

### Dashboard Widgets
🔶 **Upcoming Events Configuration** (Status: PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Shows upcoming events
- **Missing:** Configuration for "how far out" or "how many events"
- **Recommendation:** Show next 5 events regardless of date
- **File:** src/widgets/Dashboard/UpcomingEventsWidget.tsx

🔶 **Recent Notes Timeframe** (Status: PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Shows 5 most recently updated (with subtitle)
- **Missing:** Configurable timeframe filter
- **Question:** Should filter to last 24h only?
- **Recommendation:** Make timeframe configurable

### Notes System
🔶 **Notes UI Polish** (Status: PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Functional but "unpolished and childish feeling" (user feedback)
- **Missing:** Many modern note-taking features
- **Research Needed:** See [Research Needed](#research-needed) section

### Forms & Data Collection
🔶 **Form Builder** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Google Forms, Typeform, Tally
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ 11 field types: text, textarea, number, date, time, select, multiselect, radio, checkbox, rating, scale
  - ✅ Field validation (required, min/max for numbers)
  - ✅ Drag-to-reorder fields (up/down buttons)
  - ✅ Field duplication and deletion
  - ✅ Form settings (title, description, allowMultipleSubmissions, resetPeriod)
  - ✅ Dynamic form rendering (FormRenderer component)
  - ✅ Client-side validation with error messages
  - ✅ Response storage with timestamps
  - ✅ Response viewing and CSV export
  - ✅ Forms Dashboard Widget (stats, top forms, navigation)
  - ✅ Form duplication
  - ✅ Success state after submission
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Conditional Logic/Branching** - Can't show/hide fields based on answers
     - Google Forms: ✅ Section-based branching
     - Typeform: ✅ Advanced logic with scoring, calculations
     - Tally: ✅ FREE intuitive block-based logic builder
     - **Impact:** Can't create dynamic forms that adapt to responses - major limitation for complex workflows
  2. ❌ **File Upload** - Can't collect documents, images, resumes
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ File upload capability
     - **Impact:** Unusable for job applications, document collection, portfolio submissions - baseline feature
  3. ❌ **Progress Bar** - Users can't see how far through form they are
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Show progress indicator
     - **Impact:** Higher abandon rates on long forms - standard UX practice
  4. ❌ **Response Analytics** - No insights into form performance
     - Google Forms: ✅ Basic analytics
     - Typeform: ✅ Advanced analytics (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Response analytics
     - **Impact:** Can't optimize forms or understand user behavior
  5. ❌ **Spam Protection** - No reCAPTCHA or honeypot
     - Typeform: ✅ Spam protection (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Built-in spam protection
     - **Impact:** Vulnerable to spam submissions
  6. ❌ **Answer Piping** - Can't reference previous answers in questions
     - Typeform: ✅ Answer piping (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ FREE answer piping
     - **Impact:** Can't create personalized form experiences
  7. ❌ **Hidden Fields** - No UTM parameter tracking
     - Typeform: ✅ Hidden fields (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ FREE hidden fields for URL params
     - **Impact:** Can't track marketing campaigns via forms
  8. ❌ **Quiz Mode** - No auto-scoring or feedback per answer
     - Google Forms: ✅ Quiz mode with auto-scoring
     - Typeform: ✅ Quiz mode (paid)
     - Tally: ✅ Quiz features
     - **Impact:** Can't create assessments or knowledge tests
  9. ❌ **Custom Thank You Page** - No redirect after submission
     - Google Forms: ✅ Custom confirmation message
     - Typeform: ✅ Custom thank you page
     - Tally: ✅ Redirect after submission
     - **Impact:** Can't guide users to next action
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ FREE (Typeform charges for logic, scoring, hidden fields)
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ Clean, minimal UI
  - ✅ Well-typed TypeScript implementation
  - ✅ No data on external servers (privacy)
- **Files:** `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx`, `src/components/FormRenderer/FormRenderer.tsx`, `src/types/forms.ts`, `src/stores/useFormsStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Conditional logic, File upload, Progress bar, Analytics, Spam protection), **P1** (Answer piping, Hidden fields, Quiz mode)
- **Verdict:** MVP-level form builder with core functionality, but missing advanced features users expect from modern form tools - Tally offers more features for FREE
- **Sources:** [Typeform vs Tally](https://www.typeform.com/blog/typeform-vs-tally), [Google Forms Features](https://clickydrip.com/google-forms-features/), [Form Builder Must-Haves](https://www.thehumancapitalhub.com/articles/the-7-best-form-builder-tools-for-20252026)

### Diagramming & Visual Tools
🔶 **Diagram Editor** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Excalidraw, Draw.io (diagrams.net), Lucidchart
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Konva-based canvas rendering (~50KB library)
  - ✅ 11 shape types: rectangle, circle, ellipse, triangle, diamond, line, arrow, text, freehand, connector (straight/curved/orthogonal)
  - ✅ Drag-to-move shapes with physics
  - ✅ Resize/rotate shapes with transform handles
  - ✅ Multi-select via Shift+click
  - ✅ Undo/Redo (50-step history)
  - ✅ Copy/Paste (Ctrl+C/V)
  - ✅ Delete (Delete key)
  - ✅ Z-index management (bring to front/send to back)
  - ✅ Canvas zoom/pan (0.1x-4x scale)
  - ✅ Grid background with snap-to-grid (20px, configurable)
  - ✅ Shape styling (fill, stroke, strokeWidth, strokeStyle)
  - ✅ Text formatting (fontSize, fontWeight, textAlign)
  - ✅ Smart connectors (attach to shapes)
  - ✅ Export options (PNG, SVG, PDF, JSON)
  - ✅ Auto-save (2s debounce)
  - ✅ Keyboard shortcuts (Delete, Copy/Paste, Undo/Redo, Select All, Escape)
  - ✅ Freehand drawing tool
  - ✅ Arrow markers (start/end arrows)
  - ✅ Full-screen editor at `/diagrams/:id`
  - ✅ Library view with grid/list modes, search, create/delete
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Shape Libraries** - No pre-built shapes (flowchart, UML, network, AWS, etc.)
     - Draw.io: ✅ 1000+ shapes across 50+ categories (flowcharts, UML, network diagrams, cloud architecture)
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Extensive professional shape libraries
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Built-in shape library
     - **Impact:** Users must draw everything from basic shapes - tedious for professional diagrams
  2. ❌ **Templates** - No starting points for common diagrams
     - Draw.io: ✅ 100+ free templates
     - Lucidchart: ✅ 1000+ templates (flowcharts, org charts, wireframes, network diagrams)
     - **Impact:** Slow to create standard diagram types, no best practices guidance
  3. ❌ **Grouping** - Can't group shapes to move as a unit
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Support shape grouping/ungrouping
     - **Impact:** Managing complex diagrams with many shapes is painful - baseline feature
  4. ❌ **Alignment Tools** - No align left/center/right/top/bottom or distribute evenly
     - Draw.io & Lucidchart: ✅ Full alignment/distribution toolbar
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Alignment tools
     - **Impact:** Manual pixel-perfect alignment required, unprofessional-looking diagrams
  5. ❌ **Layers** - Only basic z-index, no layer management
     - Draw.io: ✅ Layer panel with show/hide/lock
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Layer management
     - **Impact:** Hard to organize complex diagrams with many overlapping elements
  6. ❌ **Auto-Layout** - No automatic arrangement for flowcharts/org charts
     - Draw.io: ✅ Auto-layout algorithms
     - Lucidchart: ✅ Smart container auto-resize
     - **Impact:** Manual layout adjustments when structure changes
  7. ❌ **Hand-drawn Style** - No Excalidraw-style sketchy rendering
     - Excalidraw: ✅ Signature hand-drawn aesthetic
     - **Impact:** All diagrams look formal/rigid, no casual brainstorming style
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Freehand drawing (Draw.io doesn't have this)
  - ✅ Clean, fast Konva rendering
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with 50 steps
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ FREE (Lucidchart is paid, $7.95/mo+)
  - ✅ Privacy-focused (no cloud, no tracking)
  - ✅ Custom-built per CLAUDE.md "Build vs Buy" philosophy
- **Files:** `src/pages/DiagramEditor.tsx`, `src/components/DiagramCanvas/Canvas.tsx`, `src/types/diagrams.ts`, `src/stores/useDiagramEditorStore.ts`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Shape libraries, Grouping, Alignment tools), **P1** (Templates, Auto-layout, Layers), **P2** (Hand-drawn style)
- **Verdict:** Canvas editing works well with unique freehand feature, but missing shape libraries makes it impractical for professional diagrams - can't compete without pre-built shapes
- **Sources:** [Excalidraw Features 2024](https://plus.excalidraw.com/blog/excalidraw-in-2024), [Diagramming Software Comparison](https://zapier.com/blog/flowchart-diagramming-software/), [Lucidchart Features](https://www.lucidchart.com/pages)

### Time Tracking
🔶 **Time Tracking System** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Toggl Track, Clockify, Harvest
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Timer tab with start/stop/pause functionality
  - ✅ Manual time entry creation
  - ✅ Project assignment
  - ✅ Description field for entries
  - ✅ Time entry list with edit/delete
  - ✅ Calendar view of time entries (integrated with events)
  - ✅ Summary/reports tab (monthly time report)
  - ✅ Projects management UI
  - ✅ Time entries grouped by date
  - ✅ Day detail modal (click day to see entries)
  - ✅ "Add Time Entry" quick button
  - ✅ Duration calculations
  - ✅ Project color coding
  - ✅ Unified Schedule page with 5 tabs
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ❌ **Billable Hours System** - No billable/non-billable marking
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Mark entries as billable
     - Clockify: ✅ Billable/non-billable toggle FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Billable hours tracking with rates
     - **Impact:** Can't track what to invoice clients for - baseline feature for freelancers
  2. ❌ **Billable Rates** - No per-project or per-task hourly rates
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Set billable rates per project
     - Clockify: ✅ Hourly rates FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Per-person, per-project rates
     - **Impact:** Manual calculation of billable amounts - can't generate invoices
  3. ❌ **Idle Detection** - Timer runs when away from keyboard
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Idle detection with prompts
     - Clockify: ✅ Idle detection FREE
     - Harvest: ✅ Auto-stop idle timer
     - **Impact:** Inaccurate time tracking, over-billing risk
  4. ❌ **Invoicing** - No invoice generation from tracked time
     - Harvest: ✅ Comprehensive invoicing + payment acceptance
     - Clockify: ✅ One-click invoice generation
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Basic invoicing
     - **Impact:** Must use separate tool for billing - major workflow gap
  5. ❌ **Automatic Time Tracking** - No app/website monitoring
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Automatic tracking (paid)
     - Clockify: ✅ Automatic tracking FREE
     - **Impact:** Must remember to start/stop timer manually
  6. ❌ **Advanced Reporting** - Only basic monthly summary
     - ALL 3 competitors: ✅ Detailed reports with filters, exports, charts
     - **Impact:** Can't analyze productivity patterns or project profitability
  7. ❌ **Project Profitability** - No cost vs. billable analysis
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Profitability tracking
     - Harvest: ✅ Budget vs. actual, project margins
     - **Impact:** Don't know if projects are profitable
  8. ❌ **Pomodoro Integration** - No focus timer mode
     - Toggl Track: ✅ Pomodoro timer mode
     - Clockify: ✅ Pomodoro integration
     - **Impact:** Can't use for focused work sessions
- **Our Advantages:**
  - ✅ Calendar view integration (nice UX vs competitors' separate calendar)
  - ✅ Unified with events (all-in-one schedule page)
  - ✅ Offline-first, local storage
  - ✅ FREE (Toggl charges for many features, $10-20/user/mo)
  - ✅ Privacy-focused (no time data sent to cloud)
- **Files:** `src/pages/TimeTracking.tsx`, `src/components/TimeTrackingTimer.tsx`, `src/components/TimeEntryList.tsx`, `src/components/TimeEntrySummary.tsx`
- **Priority:** **P0** (Billable hours, Rates, Idle detection), **P1** (Invoicing, Advanced reports, Automatic tracking), **P2** (Pomodoro, Project profitability)
- **Verdict:** Basic time tracking works, but missing ALL professional freelancer features - can't be used for client billing, no competitive advantage vs Clockify free tier
- **Sources:** [Toggl vs Clockify vs Harvest](https://toggl.com/blog/clockify-vs-harvest), [Time Tracking Essential Features](https://thedigitalprojectmanager.com/tools/time-tracking-software/), [Idle Detection Features](https://hubstaff.com/idle-time-tracking-software)

### AI Terminal
🔶 **Phantom Shell Web Alternatives** (Status: PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Shows "not supported" for native commands
- **Missing:** Web-compatible alternatives
- **Needed Commands:**
  - `/ping` - fetch-based latency test
  - `/curl` - fetch wrapper
  - `/git` - isomorphic-git integration
  - `/python` - Pyodide integration (heavy)
  - `/docker` - N/A (cannot emulate)
  - `/ssh` - N/A (cannot emulate)

### Link Library
🔶 **Browser Bookmark Sync** (Status: RESEARCH NEEDED → PARTIAL)
- **Current:** Manual export/import
- **Desired:** Direct browser API integration
- **See:** Browser Integration section

---

## MISSING ALL-IN-ONE PLATFORM FEATURES

Based on research into Notion, Coda, Airtable, ClickUp, Monday.com - features that all-in-one platforms have that we're missing:

### Database & Data Management

**Missing Core Features:**
1. **❌ Database Views** - We have forms/tables but no:
   - Gallery view (visual cards with covers)
   - Calendar view of database items
   - Timeline view
   - Board view (Kanban from database)
   - List view with customization
   - **Impact:** Limited ways to visualize and interact with structured data

2. **❌ Relations** - Can't link databases together
   - Notion/Airtable: Link tasks to projects, contacts to companies
   - **Impact:** Data is siloed, no cross-referencing between types

3. **❌ Rollups** - Can't aggregate data from related items
   - Notion: Sum/count/average values from linked records
   - **Impact:** Manual calculation required for summaries

4. **❌ Formulas** - No computed fields
   - Notion: Formula columns for calculations
   - Airtable: Complex formulas, lookups
   - **Impact:** Static data only, no automation of calculations

5. **❌ Advanced Sorting & Filtering** - Limited compared to competitors
   - Notion: Multi-level sorting, complex filters
   - **Impact:** Can't create sophisticated views of data

### Automation Gaps

**Missing Automation Features:**
1. **❌ Visual Automation Builder** - Our automation is code/JSON only
   - Coda: Buttons that trigger workflows
   - Monday.com: No-code automation builder
   - **Impact:** Only technical users can create automations

2. **❌ Cross-Database Automations** - Can't trigger across features
   - Example: Completing task → create time entry → update project status
   - **Impact:** Manual workflow coordination required

3. **❌ Scheduled Automations** - No time-based triggers
   - Notion: Run automation daily/weekly/monthly
   - **Impact:** Can't automate recurring workflows

### Integration & Extensibility

**Missing Features:**
1. **❌ API / Webhooks** - No external integration capability
   - Notion/Coda/Airtable: REST APIs for external tools
   - **Impact:** Isolated system, can't connect to other tools

2. **❌ Import/Export Beyond Backup** - Limited data portability
   - Missing: CSV import for tasks/notes
   - Missing: Markdown export for notes
   - **Impact:** Data lock-in, migration difficulty

3. **❌ Embeds** - Can't embed external content
   - Notion: Embed Google Docs, Figma, YouTube, etc.
   - **Impact:** Must leave app to view external resources

### AI Features (2025 Standard)

**What Competitors Have:**
1. **❌ AI Writing Assistant** - No AI help with notes
   - Notion AI: Continue writing, summarize, improve writing
   - **Impact:** Manual writing only

2. **❌ AI Summaries** - Can't auto-summarize content
   - Notion: Summarize meeting notes, long documents
   - **Impact:** Must read everything manually

3. **❌ AI Task/Event Generation** - Can't extract from text
   - Notion: "Add event: Team meeting Friday 3pm" → creates event
   - **Impact:** Manual entry required

**Sources:**
- [Notion vs Coda vs Airtable](https://www.whalesync.com/blog/coda-vs-notion-the-ultimate-guide)
- [Notion Database Features](https://www.notion.com/help/relations-and-rollups)
- [Airtable Features](https://www.airtable.com/)

---

## PRODUCTIVITY WIDGET IDEAS

Based on research into user demands and competitor features:

### Financial Tracking Widgets (HIGH DEMAND - Not Implemented)

1. **❌ Expense Tracker Widget**
   - Quick expense entry with categories
   - Monthly spending by category
   - Budget vs actual comparison
   - **Competitors:** Mint, YNAB, Quicken
   - **Priority:** HIGH (common user request)

2. **❌ Savings Goals Widget**
   - Progress bars for savings goals
   - Projected completion dates
   - Visual motivation
   - **Competitors:** YNAB, EveryDollar
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

3. **❌ Net Worth Tracker Widget**
   - Assets - liabilities = net worth
   - Month-over-month change
   - Trend graph
   - **Competitors:** Personal Capital, Kubera
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

4. **❌ Investment Portfolio Widget**
   - Stock/crypto ticker integration
   - Portfolio allocation pie chart
   - Gains/losses tracking
   - **Competitors:** Personal Capital, Kubera
   - **Priority:** LOW (privacy concerns with API keys)

### Focus & Well-being Widgets

1. **✅ Pomodoro Widget** - WE HAVE THIS
   - **Status:** COMPLETE

2. **❌ Mood Tracker Widget**
   - Daily mood logging with emoji
   - Mood statistics over time
   - Correlation with habits/tasks
   - **Competitors:** Daylio, Moodfit
   - **Priority:** HIGH (mental health awareness)

3. **❌ Habit Streaks Widget**
   - Visual streak counters
   - Heatmap calendar view
   - Preset + custom habits
   - **Competitors:** Habitica, Streaks, Loop Habit Tracker
   - **Priority:** HIGH (user requests)

4. **❌ Daily Gratitude Widget**
   - Journal prompt
   - Archive of past gratitude entries
   - **Competitors:** Grateful, Day One
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

5. **❌ Focus Session Widget**
   - Deep work time blocking
   - Distraction-free mode trigger
   - Focus time analytics
   - **Competitors:** Freedom, Forest
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Productivity & Insights Widgets

1. **❌ Productivity Score Widget**
   - Tasks completed vs planned
   - Time tracked vs estimated
   - Weekly/monthly trends
   - **Competitors:** RescueTime
   - **Priority:** HIGH (analytics expansion)

2. **❌ Goal Progress Widget**
   - OKR tracking
   - Milestone progress bars
   - Deadline countdowns
   - **Competitors:** Asana Goals, ClickUp Goals
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

3. **❌ Daily/Weekly Review Widget**
   - Reflection prompts
   - What went well / what to improve
   - Wins archive
   - **Competitors:** Notion templates
   - **Priority:** MEDIUM

**Sources:**
- [Personal Dashboard Best Practices](https://blog.adenin.com/best-personal-dashboards/)
- [Finance Dashboard Widgets](https://www.kubera.com/blog/financial-dashboard)
- [Habit Tracking Apps](https://www.widgetly.co/blog/best-habit-tracking-apps)

  1461→## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE
  1462→
  1463→### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
  1464→- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
  1465→- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
  1466→- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
  1467→- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
  1468→- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
  1469→- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
  1470→- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
  1471→- **Offline:** Full offline functionality
  1472→

  1474→⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
  1475→- **Status:** Planned 2026
  1476→- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
  1477→- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)
  1478→
  1479→⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
  1480→- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
  1481→- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
  1482→- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
  1483→- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

  1486→- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
  1487→- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
  1488→- **Priority:** MEDIUM

  1494→- **Priority:** LOW (future)

  1498→- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
  1499→- **Priority:** LOW

file.content:
⬜ **Meeting Transcription**
- **API:** Web Speech API or Otter.ai-like
- **Privacy:** Consider on-device only
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Voice Memos**
- **Competitors:** Apple Voice Memos
- **Features:** Record, transcribe, search
- **Priority:** LOW

### Reminders & Alerts
⬜ **Enhanced Reminder System**
- **Competitors:** Apple Reminders, Due
- **Features:**
  - Time-based reminders
  - Location-based (if mobile)
  - Persistent notifications
  - Natural language input
  - Smart scheduling
  - Integration with calendar events
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Automation & Integration
⬜ **Workflow Automation Engine**
- **Competitors:** Zapier, IFTTT, n8n
- **Scope:** Internal automation (tasks, events, habits)
- **Status:** Planned local automation
- **Examples:**
  - "When task marked done, create next task"
  - "Every Monday 9am, create weekly review"
  - "If habit missed 3 days, send reminder"
- **Priority:** HIGH

⬜ **Browser Extension**
- **Features:**
  - Bookmark sync to Link Library
  - Web clipper (save pages to notes)
  - Quick capture
  - Tab sync with Tab Manager widget
- **Priority:** HIGH

⬜ **Integration Plugins**
- **Examples:**
  - Import from Trello/Asana/Todoist
  - Sync with external calendars
  - Export to productivity tools
- **Status:** Some import planned
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Creative Tools
⬜ **Drawing/Sketch Tool**
- **Competitors:** Microsoft Paint, Photopea
- **Features:**
  - Canvas-based drawing
  - Basic shapes and brushes
  - Color picker
  - Export to PNG/SVG
  - Inline in notes
- **Priority:** LOW

### Developer Tools
⬜ **Phantom Shell Enhancements**
- **Status:** PARTIAL (see above)
- **Missing:**
  - `/git` integration (isomorphic-git)
  - `/sql` queries (sql.js for local data)
  - `/jq` for JSON processing
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Script Editor/Macros**
- **Inspiration:** Apple Automator, Script Editor
- **Use Case:** User scripting for automation
- **Priority:** LOW

---

## RESEARCH NEEDED

### 1. Diagramming & Visual Tools
**Research Status:** Documented but not executed
**Competitors to Research:**
- Draw.io (diagrams.net) - Open source, browser-based, offline
- Microsoft Visio - Industry standard
- Lucidchart - Modern UX patterns
- Excalidraw - Local-first, hand-drawn aesthetic (BEST FIT)
- Miro - Collaborative whiteboard

**Key Questions:**
- Canvas rendering approach (SVG vs Canvas API)?
- Shape definition format?
- Export to standard formats?
- Touch/stylus support?

**Priority:** HIGH (user likely wants this)

---

### 2. Task & Project Management Deep Dive
**Research Status:** Partial (competitive analysis done)
**Remaining Research:**
- How to handle recurring task patterns?
- Natural language date parsing libraries?
- Task dependencies implementation patterns?
- Time estimation algorithms?

**Competitors Analyzed:**
- ✅ Monday.com (Done)
- ✅ ClickUp (Done)
- ✅ Asana (Done)
- ✅ Trello (Done)
- ⬜ Todoist (Not started)
- ⬜ TickTick (Not started)
- ⬜ Things 3 (Not started)

**Priority:** HIGH

---

### 3. Note-Taking Polish & Features
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Feedback:** "Unpolished and childish feeling"

**Competitors to Research:**
- Google NotebookLM - AI-powered research, cutting edge
- Microsoft OneNote - Free-form canvas, flexible
- Notion - Block-based editing, modern standard
- Obsidian - Local-first markdown, plugins (CLOSEST MATCH)
- Apple Notes - Simple, integrated UX
- Bear - Design excellence, markdown
- Roam Research - Bidirectional linking pioneer
- Logseq - Open source outliner
- Craft - Beautiful documents
- Reflect - Privacy + AI

**Key Questions:**
- What makes notes feel "polished"?
- Block-based vs document-based editing?
- Linking and backlinks implementation?
- Graph view feasibility?
- Typography improvements?
- AI features (local vs cloud)?

**Priority:** CRITICAL (user dissatisfaction noted)

---

### 4. Browser Bookmark Sync
**Research Status:** Problem identified, no solution
**User Request:** Direct sync with browser bookmarks

**Research Topics:**
- Chrome bookmarks API capabilities
- Firefox bookmarks API capabilities
- Edge bookmarks API capabilities
- Browser extension development (Manifest V3)
- Cross-browser compatibility
- IndexedDB access from extension
- Native messaging between extension and web app

**Potential Solutions:**
1. Browser extension that syncs with Neuman Brain
2. Read browser bookmark files directly (file system API)
3. Manual import/export improvement

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 5. Time Tracking Solutions
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Request:** Dedicated time tracking section in Schedule

**Competitors to Research:**
- Toggl Track - Industry leader, one-click timer
- Clockify - Free tier, team features
- Harvest - Invoicing, business features
- RescueTime - Automatic tracking
- Timeular - Physical tracker UX innovation

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs advanced?
- Reports and visualization patterns?
- Billable hours tracking?
- Automatic time suggestions?
- Pomodoro integration?

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 6. Terminal & Shell Emulation
**Research Status:** Problem identified, partial research
**User Request:** Web alternatives to native commands

**Web-Based Terminals to Research:**
- StackBlitz WebContainers
- CodeSandbox
- Replit
- Gitpod

**Commands Needing Alternatives:**
| Native | Web Alternative | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|----------------|
| ping | fetch() timing | Measure latency |
| curl | fetch() | HTTP requests |
| wget | fetch() + download | File downloads |
| git | isomorphic-git | Full git in browser |
| python | Pyodide | Python via WebAssembly |
| ssh | N/A | Cannot emulate |
| docker | N/A | Cannot emulate |

**Libraries to Research:**
- isomorphic-git - Git in JavaScript
- Pyodide - Python in WebAssembly (HEAVY)
- jq-web - JSON processing
- sql.js - SQLite in browser

**Priority:** MEDIUM

---

### 7. Spreadsheet Implementation
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Competitors to Research:**
- Microsoft Excel - Industry standard
- Google Sheets - Web-first, real-time collab
- Apple Numbers - Design-focused, canvas-based
- LibreOffice Calc - Open source
- Luckysheet / Univer - Open source browser-based
- Handsontable - JavaScript library (EMBEDDABLE)

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs nice-to-have?
- Formula support requirements?
- Use existing library or build from scratch?
- Performance with large datasets?

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 8. Database Widget Feasibility
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Competitors to Research:**
- Microsoft Access - Desktop database paradigm
- Airtable - Spreadsheet-database hybrid (BEST UX)
- Notion Databases - Inline with notes
- NocoDB - Open source Airtable alternative
- Baserow - Open source, API-first

**Key Questions:**
- Balance between power and simplicity?
- Field types to support?
- Query capabilities?
- Integration with other NB data?

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 9. Privacy-Focused Services Analysis
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**Purpose:** Learn from Proton's approach

**Services to Research:**
- Proton Drive - E2EE file storage
- Proton Notes - Encrypted notes (if exists)
- Proton Docs - Collaboration with encryption
- Proton Calendar - Encrypted events
- Proton Lumo AI - Privacy-preserving AI

**Key Questions:**
- How to implement E2EE sync?
- Privacy-first collaboration patterns?
- Secure sharing options?

**Priority:** MEDIUM (aligns with NB philosophy)

---

### 10. Real-Time Collaboration (Future)
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**Scope:** Future feature, not immediate

**Frameworks to Research:**
- Microsoft Fluid Framework - Distributed data structures

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |

- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry
