## Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)

> This section catalogs external tools that overlap with Neuman Brain’s problem space: tasks, projects, time tracking, notes/PKM, diagrams, automation, databases, and scheduling. It is intentionally broad so that future importers, integrations, and “competitive compat” feature flags have a single reference index.

### A. Open-Source / Self-Hosted Task & Project Management

#### Vikunja — self-hosted task manager

- Category: Personal + team task management, kanban, projects, self-hosted or managed cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Projects and namespaces with tasks, subtasks, labels, priorities, and due dates.
  - Multiple views: list, kanban board, Gantt, and table.
  - Recurring tasks, reminders, saved filters, and task relations.
  - Collaboration: share lists with users/teams, granular permissions.
  - CalDAV integration for calendar sync; API for automations.
- Public documentation:
  - Feature overview: https://vikunja.io/features/
  - Docs: https://vikunja.io/docs/
  - Source: https://github.com/go-vikunja/vikunja

#### OpenProject — open-source project & portfolio management

- Category: Project and portfolio management (agile + classic), on-prem or cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Work packages (tasks/issues), milestones, and versions supporting full project lifecycle.
  - Views: Gantt, boards (Scrum/Kanban), calendars, roadmaps, and lists.
  - Built-in time tracking, costs, and budgeting per work package.
  - Collaboration: wiki, documents, meetings, and forums per project.
  - Roles/permissions, multi-project portfolios, on-prem enterprise deployments.
- Public documentation:
  - Introduction: https://www.openproject.org/docs/getting-started/openproject-introduction/
  - Features: https://www.openproject.org/collaboration-software-features/
  - Docs index: https://www.openproject.org/docs/

#### Leantime — open-source PM “for non-project-managers”

- Category: Lightweight project management, idea → delivery pipeline.
- Highlights:
  - Projects with idea boards, research, tasks, milestones, and timelines.
  - Kanban and list views, simple time tracking and reporting.
  - Backlog management and roadmap views for long-term planning.
  - Designed with focus and structure aids (ADHD-friendly workflows).
- Public documentation:
  - Overview & cloud/open-source info: https://leantime.io/
  - Docs & source hub: https://leantime.io/open-source-project-management-software/

#### Jinear — task manager with calendar-centric view

- Category: Tasks + calendars with project feeds.
- Highlights:
  - Workspaces with projects and task lists for individuals and teams.
  - Tasks include reminders, comments, files, and assignees.
  - Calendar-driven planning and Google Calendar integration.
  - Public feed pages for projects, with optional custom domains.
- Public documentation:
  - Site & feature overview: https://jinear.co/
  - Setup docs (GitLab README): https://gitlab.com/140crafts/use-jinear/-/blob/main/README.md

#### Super Productivity — FOSS task manager + time tracker

- Category: Personal productivity, tasks + time tracking, desktop-first.
- Highlights:
  - Projects/tasks with prioritization, estimates, and checklists.
  - Built-in timers and time tracking per task; Pomodoro support.
  - Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, Jira, and more for importing issues.
  - Offline, local-first; no mandatory account; open-source.
- Public documentation:
  - Features & use cases: https://super-productivity.com/
  - Time tracking overview: https://super-productivity.com/use-cases/time-tracker/
  - Source: https://github.com/johannesjo/super-productivity

### B. Work-OS / Connected Databases & All-in-One Workspaces

#### Notion — all-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, and databases

- Category: Notes, docs, databases, wikis, tasks, and lightweight project management.
- Highlights:
  - Block-based pages combining text, media, and database views.
  - Relational databases with board, table, list, calendar, timeline, gallery views.
  - Inline tasks, recurring templates, and simple automations.
  - Collaboration: shared workspaces, comments, mentions, permissions.
  - Integrations and API for external tools; AI assistant for writing and summarization.
- Public documentation:
  - Help & guides: https://www.notion.so/help
  - API docs: https://developers.notion.com/docs

#### Coda — docs-as-apps platform

- Category: Document-centric work-OS combining tables, automations, and packs (integrations).
- Highlights:
  - Pages composed of rich text, tables, and views (kanban, calendar, timeline).
  - Relational data model with formulas similar to spreadsheets.
  - Automations (button actions, rules) and “Packs” for integrating external services.
  - Templates gallery for project management, CRM, OKRs, etc.
- Public documentation:
  - Learn & docs: https://coda.io/learn
  - Help center: https://help.coda.io/

#### Airtable — spreadsheet-database hybrid

- Category: Relational database with spreadsheet UI, automations, apps.
- Highlights:
  - Bases with tables, linked records, and field types (attachments, lookups, formulas, rollups).
  - Views: grid, kanban, calendar, timeline, gallery, form.
  - Automations, interfaces, and scripting blocks for workflow customization.
  - Integration ecosystem and API for external tooling.
- Public documentation:
  - Support & guides: https://support.airtable.com/
  - API docs: https://airtable.com/developers

#### ClickUp — all-in-one project management & work hub

- Category: Tasks, docs, whiteboards, goals, chat, dashboards.
- Highlights:
  - Hierarchy: workspaces, spaces, folders, lists, tasks, subtasks.
  - Multiple views: list, board, calendar, Gantt, mind map, workload.
  - Custom fields, task dependencies, recurring tasks, and automation rules.
  - Built-in docs, whiteboards, chat, and dashboards.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.clickup.com/
  - API docs: https://clickup.com/api

#### Monday.com — flexible work-OS for teams

- Category: Boards for projects, CRM, ops, and workflows.
- Highlights:
  - Boards with items and customizable columns (status, people, timeline, formulas, etc.).
  - Views: kanban, Gantt, calendar, workload, forms, dashboards.
  - Automations for status changes, notifications, and integrations.
  - Templates for project management, sales, HR, and operations.
- Public documentation:
  - Support center: https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us
  - Developer docs: https://developer.monday.com/

#### Smartsheet — spreadsheet-style project and work platform

- Category: Spreadsheet-like work management with project views.
- Highlights:
  - Sheets that can be viewed as grids, Gantt charts, calendars, and card (kanban) boards.
  - Dependencies, critical path, resource management, and reporting.
  - Forms, automation rules, and dashboards.
- Public documentation:
  - Help & learning center: https://help.smartsheet.com/

#### Fibery — company operating system (databases + docs + automations)

- Category: Work-OS with structured data, docs, and automation.
- Highlights:
  - Strong typed entities and relations for any domain (tasks, features, clients, etc.).
  - Views: board, timeline, Gantt, table, list, calendar, and more.
  - Linked documents and bi-directional relations between entities.
  - Automation rules, formulas, and rich integrations (Jira, GitHub, Slack, etc.).
- Public documentation:
  - Feature overview: https://fibery.io/features
  - Help center: https://help.fibery.io/

#### NocoDB — open-source Airtable alternative

- Category: Self-hosted spreadsheet-database platform.
- Highlights:
  - Converts existing SQL databases into smart spreadsheets.
  - Views: grid, kanban, gallery, form, calendar.
  - Field types, relations, formulas, and API auto-generation.
  - Role-based access control for teams.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.nocodb.com/

#### Baserow — open-source no-code database

- Category: Self-hosted Airtable-style database and app platform.
- Highlights:
  - Tables with field types, relations, views, and filters.
  - Form views for data collection and public sharing.
  - Plugins, automation, and REST API.
  - Hosted and self-hosted deployment options.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://baserow.io/docs

### C. Local-First / Privacy-First Knowledge Workspaces & PKM

#### Anytype — encrypted, local-first “everything app”

- Category: Personal knowledge management, docs, tasks, databases.
- Highlights:
  - Local-first architecture with encrypted sync across devices.
  - Objects and relations forming a graph of notes, tasks, and projects.
  - Spaces for grouping related objects and collaboration.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://doc.anytype.io/anytype-docs
  - Security details: https://doc.anytype.io/anytype-docs/advanced/data-and-security/how-we-keep-your-data-safe

#### AFFiNE — open-source “knowledge OS” (docs + whiteboards + DB)

- Category: Unified docs, whiteboards, and databases.
- Highlights:
  - Block-based editor that can switch between doc, board, and whiteboard modes.
  - Edgeless canvas for spatial note-taking and diagramming.
  - Local-first architecture with cloud/sync options.
- Public documentation:
  - Product & docs hub: https://affine.pro/pagedoc
  - Source: https://github.com/toeverything/AFFiNE

#### Obsidian — markdown knowledge base with plugins

- Category: Local-first markdown notes with powerful linking and plugins.
- Highlights:
  - Notes stored as plain markdown files on disk.
  - Bidirectional links, graph view, daily notes, and templates.
  - Extensive community plugin and theme ecosystem.
- Public documentation:
  - Help: https://help.obsidian.md/

#### Logseq — open-source outliner + graph

- Category: Local-first outliner for notes, tasks, and PKM.
- Highlights:
  - Daily journals with hierarchical bullet structure.
  - Bidirectional links, graph view, and queries over blocks.
  - Local storage with optional sync; open-source.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.logseq.com/

#### Roam Research — networked note-taking

- Category: Cloud-based networked thought tool.
- Highlights:
  - Daily note journal with block-level references and backlinks.
  - Graph view and query language for relationships.
  - Strong focus on research and writing workflows.
- Public documentation:
  - Help pages: https://roamresearch.com/#/app/help/page/5d3e3c0d-62c7-4f1a-8c71-9f3417c26f99

#### Tana — node-based knowledge graph

- Category: Outliner-style PKM with semantic structure.
- Highlights:
  - Everything is a node with tags and fields, forming a graph.
  - Powerful search, live queries, and views over nodes.
  - Workflows for tasks, projects, and research.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.tana.inc/

#### Heptabase — spatial thinking + notes

- Category: Whiteboard-style PKM and research platform.
- Highlights:
  - Cards and pages organized on spatial boards.
  - Backlinks and references for research projects.
  - Timelines and structures for long-term projects.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.heptabase.com/

#### Capacities — objects over pages PKM

- Category: Object-based personal knowledge and life management.
- Highlights:
  - “Objects” like notes, people, projects, and media with templates.
  - Multiple views (calendar, boards, tables) for same data.
  - Tagging and collections for semantic organization.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.capacities.io/

#### RemNote — spaced-repetition notes

- Category: PKM + flashcards with spaced repetition.
- Highlights:
  - Bi-directional linking of notes and concepts.
  - Flashcards generated from notes for spaced-repetition learning.
  - Hierarchical documents, portals, and references.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.remnote.com/

#### Amplenote — notes, tasks, calendar hybrid

- Category: Note-taking and task management with prioritization.
- Highlights:
  - Jots, notes, and tasks embedded within documents.
  - Prioritization and “weighted” tasks (Juggle mode).
  - Calendar integration and offline support.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://www.amplenote.com/help

#### Reflect — networked notes with backlinks

- Category: Personal notes, PKM, and journaling.
- Highlights:
  - Linked notes and daily journals with backlinks.
  - AI-powered search and summarization over notes and content.
  - Browser extensions and email capture.
- Public documentation:
  - Support: https://reflect.app/help

#### Craft — beautifully designed documents & workspaces

- Category: Documents and notes with rich layout and publishing.
- Highlights:
  - Block-based editor with nested pages.
  - Backlinks, daily notes, and templates.
  - Sharing and publishing to the web; Apple ecosystem apps.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://support.craft.do/

#### Standard Notes — E2EE note app

- Category: Encrypted notes, tasks, and journals.
- Highlights:
  - End-to-end encryption by default across platforms.
  - Editors, extensions, and themes; self-hosted server options.
  - Notes, tasks, and simple spreadsheets in a privacy-first model.

  - Help & docs: https://standardnotes.com/help

#### Joplin — open-source markdown notes + to-dos

- Category: Notes and to-do lists with sync and E2EE.

  - Notebooks and sub-notebooks with markdown content.

  - Help: https://joplinapp.org/help/

#### Trilium Notes — hierarchical knowledge base

- Category: Self-hosted large-scale note database.

  - Attributes, relations, and scripting for advanced workflows.
  - Web UI and desktop app; optimized for large knowledge bases.

  - Docs & source: https://github.com/TriliumNext/Trilium

#### Mem — AI-first knowledge and note workspace

#### Kosmik — visual-first PKM canvas

  1460→## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

  1462→### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
  1463→- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
  1464→- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
  1465→- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
  1466→- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
  1467→- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
  1468→- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
  1469→- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
  1470→- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

  1475→- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
  1476→- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

  1715→- **Funnel:** Import tools from Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist

  1718→- **Goal:** Match 80% of Asana/Notion features (core 20% use cases)

  1721→
  1722→### Performance

  1724→- **Current:** 60KB ✅
  1725→- **Goal:** <1s load time (95th percentile)
  1726→- **Goal:** <100ms interaction latency
  1727→
  1728→### Privacy
  1729→- **Goal:** Zero telemetry by default
  1730→- **Current:** ✅ Achieved
  1731→- **Goal:** E2EE sync by Q2 2026

  1766→## Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)

  1768→> This section catalogs external tools that overlap with Neuman Brain’s problem space: tasks, projects, time tracking, notes/PKM, diagrams, automation, databases, and scheduling. It is intentionally broad so that future importers, integrations, and “competitive compat” feature flags have a single reference index.

## RESEARCH NEEDED

### 1. Diagramming & Visual Tools
**Research Status:** Documented but not executed
**Competitors to Research:**
- Draw.io (diagrams.net) - Open source, browser-based, offline
- Microsoft Visio - Industry standard
- Lucidchart - Modern UX patterns
- Excalidraw - Local-first, hand-drawn aesthetic (BEST FIT)
- Miro - Collaborative whiteboard

**Key Questions:**
- Canvas rendering approach (SVG vs Canvas API)?
- Shape definition format?
- Export to standard formats?
- Touch/stylus support?

**Priority:** HIGH (user likely wants this)

---

### 2. Task & Project Management Deep Dive
**Research Status:** Partial (competitive analysis done)
**Remaining Research:**
- How to handle recurring task patterns?
- Natural language date parsing libraries?
- Task dependencies implementation patterns?
- Time estimation algorithms?

**Competitors Analyzed:**
- ✅ Monday.com (Done)
- ✅ ClickUp (Done)
- ✅ Asana (Done)
- ✅ Trello (Done)
- ⬜ Todoist (Not started)
- ⬜ TickTick (Not started)
- ⬜ Things 3 (Not started)

**Priority:** HIGH

---

### 3. Note-Taking Polish & Features
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Feedback:** "Unpolished and childish feeling"

**Competitors to Research:**
- Google NotebookLM - AI-powered research, cutting edge
- Microsoft OneNote - Free-form canvas, flexible
- Notion - Block-based editing, modern standard
- Obsidian - Local-first markdown, plugins (CLOSEST MATCH)
- Apple Notes - Simple, integrated UX
- Bear - Design excellence, markdown
- Roam Research - Bidirectional linking pioneer
- Logseq - Open source outliner
- Craft - Beautiful documents
- Reflect - Privacy + AI

**Key Questions:**
- What makes notes feel "polished"?
- Block-based vs document-based editing?
- Linking and backlinks implementation?
- Graph view feasibility?
- Typography improvements?
- AI features (local vs cloud)?

**Priority:** CRITICAL (user dissatisfaction noted)

---

### 4. Browser Bookmark Sync
**Research Status:** Problem identified, no solution
**User Request:** Direct sync with browser bookmarks

**Research Topics:**
- Chrome bookmarks API capabilities
- Firefox bookmarks API capabilities
- Edge bookmarks API capabilities
- Browser extension development (Manifest V3)
- Cross-browser compatibility
- IndexedDB access from extension
- Native messaging between extension and web app

**Potential Solutions:**
1. Browser extension that syncs with Neuman Brain
2. Read browser bookmark files directly (file system API)
3. Manual import/export improvement

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 5. Time Tracking Solutions
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Request:** Dedicated time tracking section in Schedule

**Competitors to Research:**
- Toggl Track - Industry leader, one-click timer
- Clockify - Free tier, team features
- Harvest - Invoicing, business features
- RescueTime - Automatic tracking
- Timeular - Physical tracker UX innovation

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs advanced?
- Reports and visualization patterns?
- Billable hours tracking?
- Automatic time suggestions?
- Pomodoro integration?

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 6. Terminal & Shell Emulation
**Research Status:** Problem identified, partial research
**User Request:** Web alternatives to native commands

**Web-Based Terminals to Research:**
- StackBlitz WebContainers
- CodeSandbox
- Replit
- Gitpod

**Commands Needing Alternatives:**
| Native | Web Alternative | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|----------------|
| ping | fetch() timing | Measure latency |
| curl | fetch() | HTTP requests |
| wget | fetch() + download | File downloads |
| git | isomorphic-git | Full git in browser |
| python | Pyodide | Python via WebAssembly |
| ssh | N/A | Cannot emulate |
| docker | N/A | Cannot emulate |

**Libraries to Research:**
- isomorphic-git - Git in JavaScript
- Pyodide - Python in WebAssembly (HEAVY)
- jq-web - JSON processing
- sql.js - SQLite in browser

**Priority:** MEDIUM

---

### 7. Spreadsheet Implementation
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Competitors to Research:**
- Microsoft Excel - Industry standard
- Google Sheets - Web-first, real-time collab
- Apple Numbers - Design-focused, canvas-based
- LibreOffice Calc - Open source
- Luckysheet / Univer - Open source browser-based
- Handsontable - JavaScript library (EMBEDDABLE)

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs nice-to-have?
- Formula support requirements?
- Use existing library or build from scratch?
- Performance with large datasets?

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 8. Database Widget Feasibility
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

---

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

### Cost Advantage
| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |
| Toggl Track | $1,080 - $2,160 |
| **Neuman Brain** | **$0** |

### Speed Advantage
- **NB:** 60KB bundle, <1s load time
- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry

---

**ALL P1 PRIORITIES SHIPPED!** 6/6 complete in single session.

### P2 - MEDIUM (3-6 months)
1. ✅ Gantt/Timeline view (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Professional timeline visualization
   - ✅ Timeline view with day/week/month zoom levels
   - ✅ Drag task bars to reschedule (updates startDate/dueDate)

3. ✅ Custom fields (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - User-defined metadata fields
   - ✅ Field definitions for tasks and notes (text/number/date/select/checkbox)
   - ✅ CRUD UI in Settings page (src/components/CustomFieldsSettings.tsx)
   - ✅ Type-aware field editor with validation (src/components/CustomFieldEditor.tsx)
   - ✅ Integration in CardDetailPanel and NotesEditor
   - ✅ Optional fields (backward compatible)

4. ✅ Tags system for notes (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26) - Comprehensive tag management
   - ✅ Tag picker with autocomplete (src/components/TagPicker.tsx)
   - ✅ Tag filter sidebar with AND logic (src/components/TagFilter.tsx)

   - ✅ Global tag operations (rename/delete across all notes)

## Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)

> This section catalogs external tools that overlap with Neuman Brain’s problem space: tasks, projects, time tracking, notes/PKM, diagrams, automation, databases, and scheduling. It is intentionally broad so that future importers, integrations, and “competitive compat” feature flags have a single reference index.

### A. Open-Source / Self-Hosted Task & Project Management

#### Vikunja — self-hosted task manager

- Category: Personal + team task management, kanban, projects, self-hosted or managed cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Projects and namespaces with tasks, subtasks, labels, priorities, and due dates.
  - Multiple views: list, kanban board, Gantt, and table.
  - Recurring tasks, reminders, saved filters, and task relations.
  - Collaboration: share lists with users/teams, granular permissions.
  - CalDAV integration for calendar sync; API for automations.
- Public documentation:
  - Feature overview: https://vikunja.io/features/
  - Docs: https://vikunja.io/docs/
  - Source: https://github.com/go-vikunja/vikunja

#### OpenProject — open-source project & portfolio management

- Category: Project and portfolio management (agile + classic), on-prem or cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Work packages (tasks/issues), milestones, and versions supporting full project lifecycle.
  - Views: Gantt, boards (Scrum/Kanban), calendars, roadmaps, and lists.
  - Built-in time tracking, costs, and budgeting per work package.
  - Collaboration: wiki, documents, meetings, and forums per project.
  - Roles/permissions, multi-project portfolios, on-prem enterprise deployments.
- Public documentation:
  - Introduction: https://www.openproject.org/docs/getting-started/openproject-introduction/
  - Features: https://www.openproject.org/collaboration-software-features/
  - Docs index: https://www.openproject.org/docs/

  - Backlog management and roadmap views for long-term planning.
  - Designed with focus and structure aids (ADHD-friendly workflows).
- Public documentation:
  - Overview & cloud/open-source info: https://leantime.io/
  - Docs & source hub: https://leantime.io/open-source-project-management-software/

#### Jinear — task manager with calendar-centric view

  - Templates for retrospectives, design sprints, etc.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://support.mural.co/

#### FigJam — Figma’s whiteboard

- Category: Whiteboarding and collaboration inside the Figma ecosystem.
- Highlights:
  - Sticky notes, stamps, widgets, and integrations with Figma designs.
  - Ideal for UX/UI teams doing early-stage ideation.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://help.figma.com/hc/en-us/sections/360011989394-FigJam

#### Lucidchart — diagramming platform

- Category: Flowcharts, org charts, network diagrams, and more.
- Highlights:
  - Rich diagram types with templates and shape libraries.
  - Data linking and automatic diagram generation.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://support.lucid.co/hc/en-us/categories/15975701660375-Lucidchart

#### draw.io / diagrams.net — free diagramming

- Category: Free, browser-based diagramming with offline options.
- Highlights:
  - Extensive shape libraries and integrations (Confluence/Jira plugins).
  - Save diagrams to local files or cloud storage.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs & tutorials: https://www.diagrams.net/doc

#### Excalidraw — hand-drawn style whiteboard

- Category: Light, open-source virtual whiteboard.
- Highlights:
  - Sketchy, hand-drawn style shapes and connectors.
  - Collaborative sessions and embeddable components.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.excalidraw.com/

#### Whimsical — visual collaboration suite

- Category: Flowcharts, wireframes, sticky notes, and documents.
- Highlights:
  - Unified interface for diagrams, docs, and whiteboards.
  - Keyboard-driven workflow and templates.
- Public documentation:
  - Help docs: https://help.whimsical.com/

#### tldraw — open-source drawing and whiteboard toolkit

- Category: Whiteboard app and embeddable drawing engine.
- Highlights:
  - Infinite canvas with shapes, text, and collaboration.
  - Open-source core suitable for embedding in apps.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://tldraw.dev/docs

### H. Automation & Workflow Orchestration

#### Zapier — SaaS automation platform

- Category: No-code automations between web apps.
- Highlights:
  - Triggers and actions across thousands of integrations.
  - Multi-step zaps, filters, and paths for complex workflows.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.zapier.com/

#### Make (formerly Integromat) — visual workflow builder

- Category: Low-code, visual workflow automation.
- Highlights:
  - Scenario builder with branching, iterators, and data mapping.
  - Deep integrations with many SaaS tools.
- Public documentation:
  - Help & docs: https://www.make.com/en/help

#### n8n — open-source workflow automation

- Category: Self-hostable automation platform.
- Highlights:
  - Node-based workflows with webhooks, triggers, and data processing.
  - Community nodes and extensibility via custom nodes.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.n8n.io/

#### IFTTT — simple applets for consumers

- Category: Simple trigger-action automations.
- Highlights:
  - “If This Then That” applets for common services and devices.
  - Focus on consumer and IoT scenarios.
- Public documentation:
  - Help: https://help.ifttt.com/

#### Bardeen — browser-centric workflow automation

- Category: Browser automation and scraping for workflows.
- Highlights:
  - Automates repetitive browser tasks and data movement.
  - Prebuilt playbooks and AI-assisted automation design.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://www.bardeen.ai/docs

### I. Wiki & Knowledge Base Systems

#### Confluence — enterprise wiki

- Category: Team documentation and knowledge base.
- Highlights:
  - Pages organized in spaces with hierarchy and labels.
  - Integrations with Jira and other Atlassian tools.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://support.atlassian.com/confluence/

#### BookStack — open-source documentation platform

- Category: Self-hosted wiki/books/chapters for docs.
- Highlights:
  - Book → chapter → page hierarchy with markdown/WYSIWYG editor.
  - Role-based permissions and simple self-hosting.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://www.bookstackapp.com/docs/

#### Wiki.js — modern open-source wiki

- Category: Node.js-based wiki with multiple backends.
- Highlights:
  - Markdown editor, diagrams, and content schemas.
  - Authentication integrations and themeable UI.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.requarks.io/

#### GitBook — docs for products and APIs

- Category: Developer and product documentation.
- Highlights:
  - Versioned documentation with Git integration.
  - Publishing with search, navigation, and access control.
- Public documentation:
  - Docs: https://docs.gitbook.com/

#### Slab — knowledge hub for teams

- Category: Hosted knowledge base with editor and search.
- Highlights:
  - Topic-based organization with fast search.
  - Integrations with Slack, GitHub, and other tools.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.slab.com/

#### Nuclino — lightweight team wiki & collaboration

- Category: Unified workspace for docs, tasks, and knowledge.
- Highlights:
  - Real-time collaborative documents with hierarchical navigation.
  - Boards and graph views for organizing content.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://www.nuclino.com/docs

#### Guru — knowledge management with browser extension

- Category: Internal knowledge base surfaced via browser cards.
- Highlights:
  - “Cards” surfaced in-context via browser extension.
  - Verification workflows and knowledge alerts.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.getguru.com/

### J. Privacy-Focused Suites & Related Services

#### Proton — encrypted email, storage, calendar, and VPN

- Category: End-to-end encrypted productivity and privacy suite.
- Highlights:
  - Proton Mail, Drive, Calendar, Pass, and VPN under one account.
  - Zero-access encryption design for content; focus on privacy.
  - Proton Lumo AI with privacy-preserving architecture.
- Public documentation:
  - Support center: https://proton.me/support
  - Security & architecture docs: https://proton.me/legal

### K. Additional Project / Issue & Work Management Suites

#### Asana — collaborative work management

- Category: Team projects, goals, workflows, work management SaaS.
- Highlights:
  - Tasks, subtasks, custom fields, tags, and dependencies.
  - Multiple views: list, board (Kanban), timeline (Gantt-like), calendar, workload.
  - Goals and portfolios to track initiatives, plus forms, rules-based automation, and templates.
  - Rich integration ecosystem (email, chat, file storage, dev tools).
- Public documentation:
  - Feature list: https://help.asana.com/s/article/all-asana-features
  - Project management guide: https://help.asana.com/s/article/managing-projects

#### Trello — Kanban-centric boards

- Category: Simple boards for projects, tasks, and personal planning.
- Highlights:
  - Boards → lists → cards workflow, with checklists, attachments, labels, and due dates.
  - Power-Ups for calendar view, custom fields, automation (Butler), and integrations.
  - Workspaces for teams, plus templates for common workflows.
- Public documentation:
  - Product tour: https://trello.com/tour
  - Getting started guide: https://trello.com/guide

#### Jira Software — agile issue & project tracking

- Category: Agile issue tracking for software and technical teams.
- Highlights:
  - Scrum and Kanban boards, sprints, epics, and backlogs.
  - Rich custom workflows, issue types, fields, and automations.
  - Reporting: burndown charts, velocity, cumulative flow, and more.
- Public documentation:
  - Jira Software agile documentation hub: https://confluence.atlassian.com/agile064
  - Scrum boards overview: https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira/features/scrum-boards

#### Wrike — enterprise work management

- Category: Work management with Gantt, dashboards, and resource planning.
- Highlights:
  - Projects, folders, and tasks with custom workflows and request forms.
  - Gantt charts, workload views, dashboards, and reporting.
  - Automation engine, approvals, proofing, and extensive integrations.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.wrike.com/hc/en-us
  - Feature overview: https://www.wrike.com/features/

#### Teamwork.com — client-service oriented PM

- Category: Project management with billing/time tracking focus.
- Highlights:
  - Projects with tasks, milestones, boards, calendar, and files.
  - Built-in time tracking, billing, and client collaboration.
  - Automations, workloads, and portfolio views.
- Public documentation:
  - Support center: https://www.teamwork.com/support/
  - Projects guides: https://support.teamwork.com/projects/guides

#### Basecamp — opinionated project hub

- Category: Message-board centric project collaboration.
- Highlights:
  - Projects with message boards, to-dos, schedules, file sharing, and check-ins.
  - Lineup view for high-level timelines across projects.
  - Strong emphasis on simplicity and asynchronous communication.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://basecamp.com/help/
  - How-to guides: https://3.basecamp-help.com/collection/109-how-to-guides

#### Zoho Projects — affordable PM suite

- Category: Cloud project management with time tracking and billing.
- Highlights:
  - Projects, milestones, task lists, issues, and dependencies with Gantt views.
  - Built-in timesheets, workload reports, and project billing.
  - Integrations across the Zoho ecosystem (Zoho Books, CRM, WorkDrive).
- Public documentation:
  - Overview: https://help.zoho.com/portal/en/kb/projects/zoho-projects-overview/articles/zoho-projects-overview
  - Knowledge base: https://help.zoho.com/portal/en/kb/projects

#### Azure Boards — DevOps project tracking

- Category: Boards and backlogs within Azure DevOps.
- Highlights:
  - Work item tracking for stories, bugs, tasks, and features.
  - Scrum, Kanban, and hybrid boards with queries and backlogs.
  - Deep integration with repos, pipelines, and artifacts.
- Public documentation:
  - Azure Boards docs: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/

#### GitHub Projects — issues + planning

- Category: Planning system layered on GitHub issues and pull requests.
- Highlights:
  - Table, board, and roadmap views powered by custom fields and filters.
  - Automation and insights built on top of GitHub issues and pull requests.
  - Project templates for common workflows.
- Public documentation:
  - About projects: https://docs.github.com/issues/planning-and-tracking-with-projects/learning-about-projects/about-projects
  - Quickstart: https://docs.github.com/issues/planning-and-tracking-with-projects/learning-about-projects/quickstart-for-projects

#### GitLab issues & boards

- Category: Issue tracking and agile boards within GitLab.
- Highlights:
  - Issues, epics, milestones, and labels for backlog management.
  - Boards for Kanban-style workflows, including swimlanes.
  - Deep integration with merge requests and CI/CD.
- Public documentation:
  - Issues & boards docs: https://docs.gitlab.com/ee/user/project/issues/

#### Shortcut — lightweight dev-focused PM

- Category: Modern project/issue tracker for product & engineering teams.
- Highlights:
  - Stories, epics, milestones, and roadmaps.
  - Kanban boards, cycles (sprints), and docs in one workspace.
  - Integrations with GitHub/GitLab, Slack, and others.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.shortcut.com/

### L. To-Do & Personal Task Apps

#### Todoist — cross-platform task manager

- Category: Personal and team to-do lists with projects.
- Highlights:
  - Projects, labels, filters, and recurring tasks.
  - Today/Upcoming views, calendar view, and natural language dates.
  - Collaboration on shared projects and comment threads.
- Public documentation:
  - Feature overview: https://todoist.com/features
  - Help center: https://todoist.com/help

#### TickTick — tasks, calendar, and habits

- Category: All-in-one to-do list with calendar and habit tracking.
- Highlights:
  - Lists, tags, and smart filters for tasks.
  - Multiple calendar views, Kanban, timeline, and Eisenhower matrix.
  - Built-in Pomodoro timer and habit tracker.
- Public documentation:
  - Feature page: https://ticktick.com/features
  - Help center: https://support.ticktick.com/

#### Microsoft To Do — simple list app

- Category: Cloud-synced lists integrated with Microsoft 365/Outlook.
- Highlights:
  - My Day, smart suggestions, and multiple lists.
  - Integration with Outlook tasks and Microsoft accounts.
  - Available on web, Windows, macOS, iOS, and Android.
- Public documentation:
  - Help & learning: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/todo
  - Welcome guide: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/welcome-to-microsoft-to-do-762cbbf9-7fc1-48e5-b619-005622da89d0

#### Things 3 — Apple-only task manager

- Category: Premium task manager for Apple platforms.
- Highlights:
  - Areas, projects, and checklists to structure life and work.
  - Today/Upcoming views and natural language input.
  - Deep integration with macOS and iOS (Shortcuts, widgets, etc.).
- Public documentation:
  - Support: https://culturedcode.com/things/support/
  - Getting productive guide: https://culturedcode.com/things/guide/

#### OmniFocus — power-user GTD system

- Category: GTD-style task manager for Apple users.
- Highlights:
  - Projects, tags, perspectives, and custom views for advanced workflows.
  - Forecast view combining tasks and calendar.
  - Powerful review tools and scripting/automation support.
- Public documentation:
  - Product site & features: https://www.omnigroup.com/omnifocus/features
  - Help & guides: https://www.omnigroup.com/omnifocus/

#### Habitica — gamified tasks & habits

- Category: Gamified habit and task tracking.
- Highlights:
  - Dailies, habits, and to-dos power an RPG-style character.
  - Parties, quests, and challenges for social accountability.
  - Open-source, with web and mobile apps.
- Public documentation:
  - Wiki: https://habitica.fandom.com/wiki/Habitica
  - App: https://habitica.com/

#### Remember The Milk — classic list manager

- Category: Long-running to-do app with smart input.
- Highlights:
  - Smart Add syntax for due dates, priorities, tags, and more.
  - Powerful search, saved lists, and keyboard shortcuts.
  - Multi-platform sync and Outlook integration via MilkSync.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://www.rememberthemilk.com/help/
  - Getting started guide: https://www.rememberthemilk.com/help/guide/

### M. Calendar & Scheduling Apps

#### Fantastical — premium calendar for Apple platforms

- Category: Calendar plus reminders/time-blocking for Apple users.
- Highlights:
  - Natural language event entry and rich calendar views.
  - Integrated tasks, weather, and conference call links.
  - Interesting calendars directory and multi-account support.
- Public documentation:
  - iOS help center: https://flexibits.com/fantastical-ios/help/getting-started
  - FAQ: https://flexibits.com/fantastical-ios/faq

#### Google Calendar — mainstream cloud calendar

- Category: Personal and team calendaring via Google Workspace.
- Highlights:
  - Multiple calendars, sharing, and access controls.
  - Event creation with conferencing, reminders, and tasks.
  - Increasing AI assistance (for example, Gemini-powered scheduling and suggestions).
- Public documentation:
  - User help: https://support.google.com/calendar/
  - Workspace training: https://support.google.com/a/users/answer/9247501

#### Outlook Calendar — Microsoft 365 calendaring

- Category: Calendar integrated with Outlook email and Microsoft 365.
- Highlights:
  - Appointments, meetings, shared calendars, and group schedules.
  - Desktop, web, and mobile clients.
  - Integration with Teams, To Do, and Microsoft 365 groups.
- Public documentation:
  - Intro to Outlook calendar: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/office/introduction-to-the-outlook-calendar-d94c5203-77c7-48ec-90a5-2e2bc10bd6f8
  - Outlook help & learning: https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/outlook

#### Notion Calendar (Cron) — integrated schedule for Notion users

- Category: Next-generation calendar now branded as Notion Calendar.
- Highlights:
  - Unified view across multiple accounts and time zones.
  - Availability sharing, team overlays, and menu bar calendar.
  - Tight integration with Notion databases and pages.
- Public documentation:
  - Product page: https://www.notion.com/product/calendar
  - Cron/Notion Calendar info: https://cron.com/

#### Calendly — scheduling links and automation

- Category: Scheduling automation platform.
- Highlights:
  - Event types with availability rules and buffers.
  - Calendar integrations (Google, Outlook, and others) plus routing logic.
  - Workflows for pre- and post-meeting email and SMS reminders.
- Public documentation:
  - Help center: https://help.calendly.com/hc/en-us
  - Developer docs and API: https://developer.calendly.com/

### N. Ecosystem Summary Matrix (Category → Tools → Neuman Brain Modules)

> Quick scanning table to connect external ecosystems to Neuman Brain’s planned modules. This is intentionally high-level; detailed parity lives in the main body above.

| Category | Example Tools | Corresponding NB Modules | Core Themes To Match/Exceed |
|---------|---------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------|
| Tasks & Projects | Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist, Vikunja | Kanban/Projects, Backlog, Roadmaps | Multi-view (list/board/calendar), dependencies, custom fields, automations |
| PKM / Notes / Graph | Obsidian, Logseq, Roam, Tana, Heptabase, Capacities | Notes, Graph View, Daily Notes, Backlinks | Local-first notes, rich linking, graph visualization, daily journaling |
| Work-OS / Databases | Notion, Coda, Airtable, Fibery, NocoDB, Baserow | Collections/Databases, Custom Fields, Views | Typed entities, relational views, formulas, templates, permissions |
| Daily Planning & Time-Blocking | Motion, Sunsama, Akiflow, Reclaim, Morgen, Fantastical | Schedule, Planner, Focus Mode | Inbox-to-calendar flow, time-blocking, capacity tracking, auto-scheduling |
| Time Tracking & Billing | Toggl Track, Clockify, Harvest, TimeCamp, Timely, Hubstaff | Time Tracking, Reporting, Billing Export | Per-task timers, timesheets, reports, client/project cost views |
| Forms & Intake | Typeform, Tally, Jotform, Google Forms, Formstack, Paperform | Forms/Intake, Data Capture, Import Pipelines | Flexible fields, branching, submissions to databases, spam controls |
| Diagrams / Whiteboards | Miro, Mural, FigJam, Lucidchart, Excalidraw, Whimsical | Whiteboard/Canvas, Diagram Widgets | Infinite canvas, templates, realtime collaboration, embeds |
| Wikis & Knowledge Base | Confluence, BookStack, Wiki.js, GitBook, Slab, Nuclino | Docs, Knowledge Base, Help Center | Hierarchical docs, search, permissions, versioning |
| Automation & Integrations | Zapier, Make, n8n, IFTTT, Bardeen, Calendly Workflows | Automation Engine, Webhooks, Integrations | Triggers/actions, visual flows, robust API plus webhooks |
| Privacy & Identity | Proton, Bitwarden, 1Password, self-hosted SSO | Account, Encryption, Permissions | End-to-end mindset, exportability, minimal data retention |

---

Use this directory when:
- Designing importers or integrations (e.g., task/time tracking import, PKM migrations, diagram export).
- Benchmarking NB features against domain-specific tools.
- Identifying future “good neighbor” features (e.g., Airtable-style databases, Obsidian-style plugins, Motion-style auto-scheduling).

  1777→## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

  1781→- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
  1782→- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
  1783→- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
  1784→- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
  1785→- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)

---

## NOT STARTED

### Time Tracking Enhancement
⬜ **Time Tracking Tab in Schedule Page**
- **Request:** Dedicated tab/section for time tracking within Schedule
- **Current:** Only accessible via sidebar
- **Benefits:** Greater customization, analytics, insights
- **Priority:** HIGH (user requested)
- **Research:** See Time Tracking Solutions section

### Task Management Advanced Features
**Note:** See PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION section above for comprehensive audits of:
- 🔶 Kanban Board (reclassified from COMPLETED - 2025-11-28)
- 🔶 Timeline/Gantt View (reclassified from COMPLETED - 2025-11-28)
- 🔶 Recurring Tasks (reclassified from COMPLETED - 2025-11-28)
- Task Dependencies (audit pending)
- Custom Fields (audit pending)

⬜ **Task Templates**
- **Competitors:** ClickUp, Asana
- **Use Case:** Repeatable workflows

⬜ **Natural Language Task Entry**
- **Competitors:** Todoist, ClickUp
- **Example:** "Meeting tomorrow at 2pm" → creates task
- **Privacy:** Can parse locally

⬜ **Subtasks/Checklist Items**
- **Competitors:** Trello, Asana, ClickUp
- **Current:** May exist in basic form
- **Enhancement:** Full nested hierarchy

⬜ **Task Time Estimation vs Actual**
- **Competitors:** ClickUp, Jira
- **Use Case:** Project planning

⬜ **Custom Task Fields**
- **Status:** Mentioned as planned
- **Competitors:** Monday.com, ClickUp, Asana (paid)
- **Implementation:** User-defined field types

⬜ **Task Automation Rules**
- **Status:** Planned local automation engine
- **Competitors:** Monday.com, Asana, Trello Butler
- **Examples:**
  - "When status → Done, notify"
  - "Daily at 9am, create standup task"
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + IndexedDB timers

⬜ **My Day Dashboard**
- **Inspiration:** Asana's "Today" view
- **Enhancement:** Calendar events + tasks + habits unified
- **Priority:** HIGH (core UX improvement)

### Calendar & Scheduling
⬜ **Recurring Events**
- **Competitors:** All calendar apps
- **Implementation:** Pattern storage in IndexedDB

⬜ **Event Reminders/Notifications**
- **Competitors:** Google Calendar, Apple Calendar
- **Implementation:** Browser notifications API

⬜ **Calendar Invites/Sharing**
- **Competitors:** Outlook, Google Calendar
- **Scope:** May conflict with local-first philosophy

⬜ **Multiple Calendars**
- **Competitors:** All calendar apps
- **Use Case:** Personal vs Work separation

⬜ **Calendar Sync** (External)
- **Competitors:** All calendar apps
- **Implementation:** Import from Google Cal, Outlook, etc.

### Notes Enhancement
⬜ **Block-Based Editor**
- **Current:** Traditional editor
- **Competitors:** Notion, Obsidian, Logseq
- **Benefits:** Flexible content blocks

✅ **Backlinks/Wikilinks** (COMPLETE - 2025-11-26)
- **Competitors:** Obsidian, Roam, Logseq, Notion
- **Implementation:** [[link]] syntax already implemented in src/utils/backlinks.ts
- **UI:** BacklinksPanel component integrated into NotesEditor
- **Status:** Production-ready, infrastructure was already complete

✅ **Graph View** (Implemented 2025-11-26 - MVP Complete)
- **Competitors:** Obsidian, Roam, Logseq
- **Use Case:** Visualize note connections
- **Implementation:** Interactive D3.js force-directed graph at /graph
- **Phase 1 Features (COMPLETE):**
  - Nodes for notes (cyan) and tags (magenta)
  - Edges from linkedNotes (backlinks)
  - Zoom, pan, drag interactions
  - Search filter, hide orphans toggle
  - Click nodes to navigate
  - Legend and instructions
  - Dark mode compatible
  - Stats display (node count, connection count)
- **Status:** MVP COMPLETE - Phase 2 enhancements optional (tag/folder filters, tooltips)

⬜ **Tags System**
- **Competitors:** Bear, Notion, Obsidian
- **Implementation:** #tag syntax

⬜ **Smart Folders/Filters**
- **Competitors:** Apple Notes, Notion
- **Examples:** "All notes modified this week"

⬜ **Note Templates**
- **Competitors:** Notion, Obsidian
- **Use Case:** Meeting notes, daily notes, etc.

⬜ **Daily Notes Pattern**
- **Competitors:** Roam, Logseq, Obsidian
- **Use Case:** Journaling, automatic daily entry

⬜ **Focus/Zen Mode**
- **Competitors:** Bear, iA Writer
- **Use Case:** Distraction-free writing

⬜ **Document Scanning**
- **Competitors:** Apple Notes, OneNote
- **Implementation:** Camera API + OCR

⬜ **Rich Text Enhancements**
- **Current:** Basic rich text (likely)
- **Missing:** Tables, code blocks, embeds
- **Competitors:** Notion, OneNote

⬜ **Note Search Improvements**
- **Current:** Basic search (likely)
- **Missing:** Full-text search, filters
- **Implementation:** Indexing layer

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Missing:** Markdown, PDF, DOCX, HTML
- **Competitors:** All note apps

### Diagramming & Visual Tools
⬜ **Mind Map Widget**
- **Competitors:** MindNode, XMind
- **Use Case:** Brainstorming, hierarchy

### Spreadsheet & Data
⬜ **Spreadsheet Widget**
- **Competitors:** Excel, Google Sheets, Airtable
- **Libraries:** Handsontable, Luckysheet, Univer
- **Features:**
  - Formulas (SUM, AVERAGE, IF, VLOOKUP)
  - Cell formatting
  - Charts
  - CSV/Excel import/export
- **Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

⬜ **Database Widget**
- **Competitors:** Airtable, Notion databases, NocoDB
- **Features:**
  - Table view
  - Field types (text, number, date, select, relation)
  - Filtering/sorting
  - Multiple views (table, cards, calendar)
- **Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

### Time Tracking Advanced
⬜ **Enhanced Time Tracking Features**
- **Competitors:** Toggl, Clockify, Harvest
- **Features:**
  - One-click timer start/stop
  - Project/client association
  - Visual reports (charts, heat maps)
  - Billable hours tracking
  - Weekly/monthly summary views
  - Export to invoice
  - Automatic time suggestions
  - Pomodoro mode integration
- **Priority:** HIGH (user requested dedicated section)

### Analytics & Reporting
✅ **Personal Analytics Dashboard** (Implemented 2025-11-26, P2 #9)
- **Status:** COMPLETE - All MVP features implemented
- **Competitors:** RescueTime, ClickUp analytics, Monday dashboards
- **Implementation:**
  - Period selection: Today/Week/Month/Year/Custom
  - 6 metric cards: Completion rate, time tracked, events, notes, overdue, productivity placeholder
  - 8 interactive charts powered by Recharts
- **Task Analytics:**
  - Completion rate trend (line chart)
  - Priority distribution (pie chart)
  - Status breakdown (bar chart)
- **Time Tracking Analytics:**
  - Time by project (bar chart)
  - Hourly distribution 0-23 (bar chart heatmap)
  - Session duration trend (line chart)
- **Calendar Analytics:**
  - Meeting vs focus time (pie chart)
- **Notes Analytics:**
  - Tag frequency (bar chart, top 10)
- **Features:**
  - Dark mode support via semantic tokens
  - Responsive layouts (mobile to desktop)
  - Empty state handling
  - Lazy-loaded widget (no initial bundle impact)
  - Memoized calculations for performance
- **Files:** 15 total (~1,200 lines of code)
  - Store: `src/stores/useAnalyticsStore.ts`
  - Utils: `src/utils/analyticsCalculations.ts`
  - Components: `src/components/Analytics/*` (10 components)
  - Widget: `src/widgets/Dashboard/AnalyticsWidget.tsx`
- **Time:** 5.25 hours (vs 10 estimated, 47% ahead of schedule)
- **Priority:** MEDIUM (✅ COMPLETE)

⬜ **Goal Tracking**
- **Competitors:** Asana Goals, ClickUp Goals
- **Features:**
  - Define goals/OKRs
  - Track progress
  - Link tasks to goals
  - Visual progress indicators
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Widgets (New)
⬜ **Calculator Widget**
- **Inspiration:** Apple Calculator
- **Features:** Basic + scientific modes, unit conversion
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Dictionary Widget**
- **API:** Free dictionary API
- **Features:** Definitions, thesaurus, word of the day
- **Privacy:** Cache locally
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **World Clock Enhancement**
- **Current:** Already exists (✅)
- **Enhancement:** Multiple cities, time zone converter
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Weather Widget**
- **API:** OpenWeatherMap or similar
- **Privacy:** User provides API key
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Habit Tracker Widget**
- **Competitors:** Habitica, Streaks, Loop Habit Tracker
- **Features:**
  - Daily check-ins
  - Streak tracking
  - Visual calendar
  - Habit chains
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Countdown/Timer Widget**
- **Features:** Multiple timers, stopwatch, countdown
- **Use Case:** Pomodoro, event countdowns
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Quote of the Day Widget**
- **Source:** Local quotes database or API
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Sticky Notes Widget**
- **Inspiration:** Apple Stickies
- **Features:** Quick notes, always visible
- **Priority:** LOW

### Presentation & Publishing
⬜ **Presentation Builder**
- **Competitors:** PowerPoint, Google Slides, Reveal.js
- **Library:** Reveal.js integration
- **Features:**
  - Markdown-based slides
  - Basic transitions
  - Presenter mode
  - Export to PDF
  - Embed in notes
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Publishing Mode**
- **Competitors:** Medium, Substack, Ghost
- **Features:**
  - Export notes to web
  - Static site generation
  - Portfolio/showcase
- **Priority:** LOW

### Security & Privacy
⬜ **Encrypted Notes Section**
- **Use Case:** Sensitive information
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user password
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Password Generator Tool**
- **Competitors:** 1Password, Bitwarden
- **Scope:** Utility, not full password manager
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **API Key Secure Storage**
- **Use Case:** Store API keys for integrations
- **Implementation:** IndexedDB encrypted storage
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Voice & Audio
⬜ **Voice Input for Notes**
- **API:** Web Speech API (browser-native)
- **Privacy:** On-device processing
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Voice Commands**
- **Examples:** "Create task...", "Open notes"
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Meeting Transcription**
- **API:** Web Speech API or Otter.ai-like
- **Privacy:** Consider on-device only
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Voice Memos**
- **Competitors:** Apple Voice Memos
- **Features:** Record, transcribe, search
- **Priority:** LOW

### Reminders & Alerts
⬜ **Enhanced Reminder System**
- **Competitors:** Apple Reminders, Due
- **Features:**

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

### Cost Advantage
| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |
| Toggl Track | $1,080 - $2,160 |
| **Neuman Brain** | **$0** |

### Speed Advantage
- **NB:** 60KB bundle, <1s load time
- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry

  1460→## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE
  1461→
  1462→### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
  1463→- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
  1464→- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
  1465→- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
  1466→- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
  1467→- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
  1468→- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
  1469→- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
  1470→- **Offline:** Full offline functionality
  1471→
  1472→### Infrastructure Gaps
  1473→⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
  1474→- **Status:** Planned 2026
  1475→- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
  1476→- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)
  1477→
  1478→⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
  1479→- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
  1480→- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
  1481→- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
  1482→- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)
  1483→
  1484→⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
  1485→- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
  1486→- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
  1487→- **Priority:** MEDIUM
  1488→
  1489→⬜ **Plugin System**
  1490→- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
  1491→- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
  1492→- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
  1493→- **Priority:** LOW (future)
  1494→
  1495→⬜ **Theme System**
  1496→- **Current:** Single theme
  1497→- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
  1498→- **Priority:** LOW
  1499→
  1500→⬜ **Export Formats**
  1501→- **Current:** .brain backup
  1502→- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
  1503→- **Priority:** MEDIUM
  1504→
  1505→⬜ **Import From Competitors**
  1506→- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
  1507→- **Status:** Some work started
  1508→- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

It is intentionally broad so that future importers, integrations, and “competitive compat” feature flags have a single reference index.

  2892→### N. Ecosystem Summary Matrix (Category → Tools → Neuman Brain Modules)
  2893→
  2894→> Quick scanning table to connect external ecosystems to Neuman Brain’s planned modules. This is intentionally high-level; detailed parity lives in the main body above.
  2895→
  2896→| Category | Example Tools | Corresponding NB Modules | Core Themes To Match/Exceed |
  2897→|---------|---------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------|
  2898→| Tasks & Projects | Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist, Vikunja | Kanban/Projects, Backlog, Roadmaps | Multi-view (list/board/calendar), dependencies, custom fields, automations |
  2899→| PKM / Notes / Graph | Obsidian, Logseq, Roam, Tana, Heptabase, Capacities | Notes, Graph View, Daily Notes, Backlinks | Local-first notes, rich linking, graph visualization, daily journaling |
  2900→| Work-OS / Databases | Notion, Coda, Airtable, Fibery, NocoDB, Baserow | Collections/Databases, Custom Fields, Views | Typed entities, relational views, formulas, templates, permissions |
  2901→| Daily Planning & Time-Blocking | Motion, Sunsama, Akiflow, Reclaim, Morgen, Fantastical | Schedule, Planner, Focus Mode | Inbox-to-calendar flow, time-blocking, capacity tracking, auto-scheduling |
  2902→| Time Tracking & Billing | Toggl Track, Clockify, Harvest, TimeCamp, Timely, Hubstaff | Time Tracking, Reporting, Billing Export | Per-task timers, timesheets, reports, client/project cost views |
  2903→| Forms & Intake | Typeform, Tally, Jotform, Google Forms, Formstack, Paperform | Forms/Intake, Data Capture, Import Pipelines | Flexible fields, branching, submissions to databases, spam controls |
  2904→| Diagrams / Whiteboards | Miro, Mural, FigJam, Lucidchart, Excalidraw, Whimsical | Whiteboard/Canvas, Diagram Widgets | Infinite canvas, templates, realtime collaboration, embeds |
  2905→| Wikis & Knowledge Base | Confluence, BookStack, Wiki.js, GitBook, Slab, Nuclino | Docs, Knowledge Base, Help Center | Hierarchical docs, search, permissions, versioning |
  2906→| Automation & Integrations | Zapier, Make, n8n, IFTTT, Bardeen, Calendly Workflows | Automation Engine, Webhooks, Integrations | Triggers/actions, visual flows, robust API plus webhooks |
  2907→| Privacy & Identity | Proton, Bitwarden, 1Password, self-hosted SSO | Account, Encryption, Permissions | End-to-end mindset, exportability, minimal data retention |
  2908→

file.content:
- **API:** Web Speech API or Otter.ai-like
- **Privacy:** Consider on-device only
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Voice Memos**
- **Competitors:** Apple Voice Memos
- **Features:** Record, transcribe, search
- **Priority:** LOW

### Reminders & Alerts
⬜ **Enhanced Reminder System**
- **Competitors:** Apple Reminders, Due
- **Features:**
  - Time-based reminders
  - Location-based (if mobile)
  - Persistent notifications
  - Natural language input
  - Smart scheduling
  - Integration with calendar events
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Automation & Integration
⬜ **Workflow Automation Engine**
- **Competitors:** Zapier, IFTTT, n8n
- **Scope:** Internal automation (tasks, events, habits)
- **Status:** Planned local automation
- **Examples:**
  - "When task marked done, create next task"
  - "Every Monday 9am, create weekly review"
  - "If habit missed 3 days, send reminder"
- **Priority:** HIGH

⬜ **Browser Extension**
- **Features:**
  - Bookmark sync to Link Library
  - Web clipper (save pages to notes)
  - Quick capture
  - Tab sync with Tab Manager widget
- **Priority:** HIGH

⬜ **Integration Plugins**
- **Examples:**
  - Import from Trello/Asana/Todoist
  - Sync with external calendars
  - Export to productivity tools
- **Status:** Some import planned
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

### Creative Tools
⬜ **Drawing/Sketch Tool**
- **Competitors:** Microsoft Paint, Photopea
- **Features:**
  - Canvas-based drawing
  - Basic shapes and brushes
  - Color picker
  - Export to PNG/SVG
  - Inline in notes
- **Priority:** LOW

### Developer Tools
⬜ **Phantom Shell Enhancements**
- **Status:** PARTIAL (see above)
- **Missing:**
  - `/git` integration (isomorphic-git)
  - `/sql` queries (sql.js for local data)
  - `/jq` for JSON processing
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Script Editor/Macros**
- **Inspiration:** Apple Automator, Script Editor
- **Use Case:** User scripting for automation
- **Priority:** LOW

---

## RESEARCH NEEDED

### 1. Diagramming & Visual Tools
**Research Status:** Documented but not executed
**Competitors to Research:**
- Draw.io (diagrams.net) - Open source, browser-based, offline
- Microsoft Visio - Industry standard
- Lucidchart - Modern UX patterns
- Excalidraw - Local-first, hand-drawn aesthetic (BEST FIT)
- Miro - Collaborative whiteboard

**Key Questions:**
- Canvas rendering approach (SVG vs Canvas API)?
- Shape definition format?
- Export to standard formats?
- Touch/stylus support?

**Priority:** HIGH (user likely wants this)

---

### 2. Task & Project Management Deep Dive
**Research Status:** Partial (competitive analysis done)
**Remaining Research:**
- How to handle recurring task patterns?
- Natural language date parsing libraries?
- Task dependencies implementation patterns?
- Time estimation algorithms?

**Competitors Analyzed:**
- ✅ Monday.com (Done)
- ✅ ClickUp (Done)
- ✅ Asana (Done)
- ✅ Trello (Done)
- ⬜ Todoist (Not started)
- ⬜ TickTick (Not started)
- ⬜ Things 3 (Not started)

**Priority:** HIGH

---

### 3. Note-Taking Polish & Features
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Feedback:** "Unpolished and childish feeling"

**Competitors to Research:**
- Google NotebookLM - AI-powered research, cutting edge
- Microsoft OneNote - Free-form canvas, flexible
- Notion - Block-based editing, modern standard
- Obsidian - Local-first markdown, plugins (CLOSEST MATCH)
- Apple Notes - Simple, integrated UX
- Bear - Design excellence, markdown
- Roam Research - Bidirectional linking pioneer
- Logseq - Open source outliner
- Craft - Beautiful documents
- Reflect - Privacy + AI

**Key Questions:**
- What makes notes feel "polished"?
- Block-based vs document-based editing?
- Linking and backlinks implementation?
- Graph view feasibility?
- Typography improvements?
- AI features (local vs cloud)?

**Priority:** CRITICAL (user dissatisfaction noted)

---

### 4. Browser Bookmark Sync
**Research Status:** Problem identified, no solution
**User Request:** Direct sync with browser bookmarks

**Research Topics:**
- Chrome bookmarks API capabilities
- Firefox bookmarks API capabilities
- Edge bookmarks API capabilities
- Browser extension development (Manifest V3)
- Cross-browser compatibility
- IndexedDB access from extension
- Native messaging between extension and web app

**Potential Solutions:**
1. Browser extension that syncs with Neuman Brain
2. Read browser bookmark files directly (file system API)
3. Manual import/export improvement

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 5. Time Tracking Solutions
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**User Request:** Dedicated time tracking section in Schedule

**Competitors to Research:**
- Toggl Track - Industry leader, one-click timer
- Clockify - Free tier, team features
- Harvest - Invoicing, business features
- RescueTime - Automatic tracking
- Timeular - Physical tracker UX innovation

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs advanced?
- Reports and visualization patterns?
- Billable hours tracking?
- Automatic time suggestions?
- Pomodoro integration?

**Priority:** HIGH (user requested)

---

### 6. Terminal & Shell Emulation
**Research Status:** Problem identified, partial research
**User Request:** Web alternatives to native commands

**Web-Based Terminals to Research:**
- StackBlitz WebContainers
- CodeSandbox
- Replit
- Gitpod

**Commands Needing Alternatives:**
| Native | Web Alternative | Implementation |
|--------|----------------|----------------|
| ping | fetch() timing | Measure latency |
| curl | fetch() | HTTP requests |
| wget | fetch() + download | File downloads |
| git | isomorphic-git | Full git in browser |
| python | Pyodide | Python via WebAssembly |
| ssh | N/A | Cannot emulate |
| docker | N/A | Cannot emulate |

**Libraries to Research:**
- isomorphic-git - Git in JavaScript
- Pyodide - Python in WebAssembly (HEAVY)
- jq-web - JSON processing
- sql.js - SQLite in browser

**Priority:** MEDIUM

---

### 7. Spreadsheet Implementation
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Competitors to Research:**
- Microsoft Excel - Industry standard
- Google Sheets - Web-first, real-time collab
- Apple Numbers - Design-focused, canvas-based
- LibreOffice Calc - Open source
- Luckysheet / Univer - Open source browser-based
- Handsontable - JavaScript library (EMBEDDABLE)

**Key Questions:**
- Essential features vs nice-to-have?
- Formula support requirements?
- Use existing library or build from scratch?
- Performance with large datasets?

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 8. Database Widget Feasibility
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched

**Competitors to Research:**
- Microsoft Access - Desktop database paradigm
- Airtable - Spreadsheet-database hybrid (BEST UX)
- Notion Databases - Inline with notes
- NocoDB - Open source Airtable alternative
- Baserow - Open source, API-first

**Key Questions:**
- Balance between power and simplicity?
- Field types to support?
- Query capabilities?
- Integration with other NB data?

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 9. Privacy-Focused Services Analysis
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**Purpose:** Learn from Proton's approach

**Services to Research:**
- Proton Drive - E2EE file storage
- Proton Notes - Encrypted notes (if exists)
- Proton Docs - Collaboration with encryption
- Proton Calendar - Encrypted events
- Proton Lumo AI - Privacy-preserving AI

**Key Questions:**
- How to implement E2EE sync?
- Privacy-first collaboration patterns?
- Secure sharing options?

**Priority:** MEDIUM (aligns with NB philosophy)

---

### 10. Real-Time Collaboration (Future)
**Research Status:** Competitors identified, not researched
**Scope:** Future feature, not immediate

**Frameworks to Research:**
- Microsoft Fluid Framework - Distributed data structures
- Yjs - CRDT-based, multiple providers (BEST FIT)
- Automerge - CRDT library, local-first

**Key Questions:**
- How to sync with local-first architecture?
- Conflict resolution patterns?
- P2P vs server-based?

**Priority:** LOW (future consideration)

---

### 11. Apple Ecosystem Deep Dive
**Research Status:** Apps listed, not researched
**Purpose:** Identify features worth adopting

**High Priority Apps:**
- Freeform - Infinite canvas, collaboration
- Reminders - Smart lists, location triggers
- Shortcuts - Automation workflows
- Keychain - Password management patterns
- Voice Memos - Audio recording
- Stickies - Quick notes

**Low Priority/N/A:**
- Health apps, system utilities, specialized tools

**Priority:** LOW-MEDIUM

---

### 12. AI Integration Patterns

## FOUNDATION & INFRASTRUCTURE

### Current Foundation (✅ Completed)
- **Tech Stack:** React 19, TypeScript 5.9, Vite 7, Zustand 5, Tailwind 3, IndexedDB
- **Architecture:** Local-first, zero-cloud by default, privacy-first
- **Data Layer:** IndexedDB with Zustand state management
- **Validation:** Zod schemas for data integrity
- **Error Handling:** Error boundaries, recovery UI
- **Import/Export:** .brain format, ICS import, CSV export (planned)
- **Performance:** 60KB bundle size (vs 2-4MB competitors)
- **Offline:** Full offline functionality

### Infrastructure Gaps
⬜ **End-to-End Encryption for Sync**
- **Status:** Planned 2026
- **Implementation:** WebCrypto API, user keys
- **Priority:** HIGH (privacy differentiator)

⬜ **Service Worker for Offline Automation**
- **Use Case:** Run automations without browser open
- **Examples:** Daily task creation, reminders
- **Implementation:** Service Worker + Background Sync API
- **Priority:** HIGH (for automation features)

⬜ **IndexedDB Query Optimization**
- **Current:** Basic IndexedDB usage
- **Needed:** Compound indexes, full-text search
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Plugin System**
- **Inspiration:** Obsidian plugins
- **Use Case:** User-created extensions
- **Implementation:** Sandboxed JavaScript API
- **Priority:** LOW (future)

⬜ **Theme System**
- **Current:** Single theme
- **Needed:** Multiple themes, user-created
- **Priority:** LOW

⬜ **Export Formats**
- **Current:** .brain backup
- **Needed:** JSON, Markdown, CSV, PDF
- **Priority:** MEDIUM

⬜ **Import From Competitors**
- **Planned:** Trello, Asana, Todoist
- **Status:** Some work started
- **Priority:** HIGH (acquisition funnel)

## MARKET POSITIONING

### Neuman Brain's Blue Ocean Opportunity
**No competitor combines:**
1. ✅ Professional project management tools
2. ⬜ Personal habit tracking + gamification
3. ✅ Local-first privacy (no forced login)
4. ⬜ Offline automation
5. ✅ 100% free (no paywalls)
6. ✅ Fast (60KB vs 2-4MB)

### Cost Advantage
| Competitor | Annual Cost (10 users) |
|------------|------------------------|
| Monday.com | $1,440 - $2,400 |
| ClickUp | $1,080 - $2,280 |
| Asana | $1,620 - $3,660 |
| Todoist | $480 - $840 |
| Toggl Track | $1,080 - $2,160 |
| **Neuman Brain** | **$0** |

### Speed Advantage
- **NB:** 60KB bundle, <1s load time
- **Competitors:** 2-4MB, 3-10s load time
- **Result:** 10x-40x faster

### Privacy Advantage
- **NB:** Local-first by default, no tracking, optional E2EE sync
- **Competitors:** Cloud-only, data on their servers, telemetry

## Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)

> This section catalogs external tools that overlap with Neuman Brain’s problem space: tasks, projects, time tracking, notes/PKM, diagrams, automation, databases, and scheduling. It is intentionally broad so that future importers, integrations, and “competitive compat” feature flags have a single reference index.

### A. Open-Source / Self-Hosted Task & Project Management

#### Vikunja — self-hosted task manager

- Category: Personal + team task management, kanban, projects, self-hosted or managed cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Projects and namespaces with tasks, subtasks, labels, priorities, and due dates.
  - Multiple views: list, kanban board, Gantt, and table.
  - Recurring tasks, reminders, saved filters, and task relations.
  - Collaboration: share lists with users/teams, granular permissions.
  - CalDAV integration for calendar sync; API for automations.
- Public documentation:
  - Feature overview: https://vikunja.io/features/
  - Docs: https://vikunja.io/docs/
  - Source: https://github.com/go-vikunja/vikunja

#### OpenProject — open-source project & portfolio management

- Category: Project and portfolio management (agile + classic), on-prem or cloud.
- Highlights:
  - Work packages (tasks/issues), milestones, and versions supporting full project lifecycle.
  - Views: Gantt, boards (Scrum/Kanban), calendars, roadmaps, and lists.
  - Built-in time tracking, costs, and budgeting per work package.
  - Collaboration: wiki, documents, meetings, and forums per project.
  - Roles/permissions, multi-project portfolios, on-prem enterprise deployments.
- Public documentation:
  - Introduction: https://www.openproject.org/docs/getting-started/openproject-introduction/
  - Features: https://www.openproject.org/collaboration-software-features/
  - Docs index: https://www.openproject.org/docs/

#### Leantime — open-source PM “for non-project-managers”

- Category: Lightweight project management, idea → delivery pipeline.
- Highlights:
  - Projects with idea boards, research, tasks, milestones, and timelines.
  - Kanban and list views, simple time tracking and reporting.
  - Backlog management and roadmap views for long-term planning.
  - Designed with focus and structure aids (ADHD-friendly workflows).
- Public documentation:
  - Overview & cloud/open-source info: https://leantime.io/
  - Docs & source hub: https://leantime.io/open-source-project-management-software/

#### Jinear — task manager with calendar-centric view

- Category: Tasks + calendars with project feeds.
- Highlights:
  - Workspaces with projects and task lists for individuals and teams.
  - Tasks include reminders, comments, files, and assignees.
  - Calendar-driven planning and Google Calendar integration.
  - Public feed pages for projects, with optional custom domains.
- Public documentation:
  - Site & feature overview: https://jinear.co/
  - Setup docs (GitLab README): https://gitlab.com/140crafts/use-jinear/-/blob/main/README.md

#### Super Productivity — FOSS task manager + time tracker

- Category: Personal productivity, tasks + time tracking, desktop-first.
- Highlights:
  - Projects/tasks with prioritization, estimates, and checklists.
  - Built-in timers and time tracking per task; Pomodoro support.
  - Integrations: GitHub, GitLab, Jira, and more for importing issues.
  - Offline, local-first; no mandatory account; open-source.
- Public documentation:
  - Features & use cases: https://super-productivity.com/
  - Time tracking overview: https://super-productivity.com/use-cases/time-tracker/
  - Source: https://github.com/johannesjo/super-productivity

### B. Work-OS / Connected Databases & All-in-One Workspaces

#### Notion — all-in-one workspace for notes, tasks, and databases

- Category: Notes, docs, databases, wikis, tasks, and lightweight project management.
- Highlights:
  - Block-based pages combining text, media, and database views.

### N. Ecosystem Summary Matrix (Category → Tools → Neuman Brain Modules)

> Quick scanning table to connect external ecosystems to Neuman Brain’s planned modules. This is intentionally high-level; detailed parity lives in the main body above.

| Category | Example Tools | Corresponding NB Modules | Core Themes To Match/Exceed |
|---------|---------------|--------------------------|-----------------------------|
| Tasks & Projects | Monday, ClickUp, Asana, Trello, Todoist, Vikunja | Kanban/Projects, Backlog, Roadmaps | Multi-view (list/board/calendar), dependencies, custom fields, automations |
| PKM / Notes / Graph | Obsidian, Logseq, Roam, Tana, Heptabase, Capacities | Notes, Graph View, Daily Notes, Backlinks | Local-first notes, rich linking, graph visualization, daily journaling |
| Work-OS / Databases | Notion, Coda, Airtable, Fibery, NocoDB, Baserow | Collections/Databases, Custom Fields, Views | Typed entities, relational views, formulas, templates, permissions |
| Daily Planning & Time-Blocking | Motion, Sunsama, Akiflow, Reclaim, Morgen, Fantastical | Schedule, Planner, Focus Mode | Inbox-to-calendar flow, time-blocking, capacity tracking, auto-scheduling |
| Time Tracking & Billing | Toggl Track, Clockify, Harvest, TimeCamp, Timely, Hubstaff | Time Tracking, Reporting, Billing Export | Per-task timers, timesheets, reports, client/project cost views |
| Forms & Intake | Typeform, Tally, Jotform, Google Forms, Formstack, Paperform | Forms/Intake, Data Capture, Import Pipelines | Flexible fields, branching, submissions to databases, spam controls |
| Diagrams / Whiteboards | Miro, Mural, FigJam, Lucidchart, Excalidraw, Whimsical | Whiteboard/Canvas, Diagram Widgets | Infinite canvas, templates, realtime collaboration, embeds |
| Wikis & Knowledge Base | Confluence, BookStack, Wiki.js, GitBook, Slab, Nuclino | Docs, Knowledge Base, Help Center | Hierarchical docs, search, permissions, versioning |
| Automation & Integrations | Zapier, Make, n8n, IFTTT, Bardeen, Calendly Workflows | Automation Engine, Webhooks, Integrations | Triggers/actions, visual flows, robust API plus webhooks |
| Privacy & Identity | Proton, Bitwarden, 1Password, self-hosted SSO | Account, Encryption, Permissions | End-to-end mindset, exportability, minimal data retention |
